AUT.personality: Use all your wits and wiles to win. You may choose whatever personality, strategy & approach that you think will be most effective.
journal:
S1901M Negotiation summary: No prior moves - this is the opening spring 1901 phase.
Productive opening negotiations. ITA agreed to a non-aggression pact: they move south toward TUN/ION, I move F TRI to ALB. Both sides promised to respect each other's home centers. TUR agreed to take BUL/GRE while I take SER/RUM - clean Balkan division. TUR also warned me that RUS intends to contest RUM with F SEV this spring. RUS confirmed they will keep WAR out of GAL but explicitly claimed RUM as their target and asked me not to contest it - I gave no such promise. GER proposed an Austro-German axis, asked me to keep out of Bohemia, and offered vague support for my Balkan moves. I told everyone my intended moves: A BUD to SER, A VIE to GAL, F TRI to ALB. Key tension: RUS wants RUM, I want RUM. Moving A VIE to GAL this turn positions me for RUM next year via BUD->RUM path. The RUS claim on RUM is a direct conflict with my interests - I must plan to contest it in F1901 or S1902. ITA's claim that A VEN moves to Tyrolia is suspicious - that threatens VIE/TRI indirectly. Will monitor. My stated moves are genuine and strategically sound regardless.

S1901M Intent going forward: Execute: A BUD -> SER, A VIE -> GAL, F TRI -> ALB. This secures SER (a supply center), positions GAL army to support RUM push next fall, and puts fleet in position to threaten GRE or support Adriatic operations. The GAL move is both defensive (watching RUS) and offensive (future RUM support). I told RUS I'm moving to GAL 'defensively' - in reality it sets up RUM contest in F1901. I will NOT concede RUM to Russia despite their request. Turkey is a useful partner for now but will become a rival once we both grow. ITA seems cooperative but Tyrolia move bears watching. GER's A BER to Silesia is slightly concerning - near GAL - but probably aimed at Russia/Poland. Maintain all relationships as neutral-warm for now while positioning aggressively.

F1901M Negotiation summary: Spring 1901 went perfectly: A BUD-SER secured Serbia, A VIE-GAL positioned me in Galicia, F TRI-ALB set up for Aegean access. No builds this round as I only hold my original 3 centers, but I'm well-positioned for fall gains. Russia grabbed RUM with F SEV, Turkey took BUL, Italy moved aggressively into TYR and ION.
Tense negotiations on all fronts. Russia flatly refused to yield RUM, threatening war and Turkey coordination if I bounce them - a credible threat I must take seriously. Turkey warned me off GRE, claiming it as their sphere and threatening conflict if F ALB moves there. Italy was cooperative, confirming A TYR holds and F ION goes to TUN, but hinted at reassessing if I expand without sharing. Germany asked about Italy in TYR and suggested joint pressure on Italy in 1902. Key tensions: both Russia and Turkey are blocking my natural expansion paths simultaneously. I promised Russia I'd keep GAL out of UKR/WAR; I told Turkey I was moving F ALB to GRE (which they rejected); I told Russia I was moving A GAL to RUM (which they will bounce). I'm caught between two hostile neighbors. Must reassess: a bounce on RUM wastes a move, and a clash with Turkey over GRE creates two enemies at once. The pragmatic play is to take GRE with F ALB (Turkey can't stop it if A BUL is moving to GRE - we'd bounce each other, but I have fleet advantage in ALB), hold SER, and accept the RUM bounce to deny Russia a free hold. Actually: if A BUL moves to GRE and F ALB moves to GRE, we bounce - neither gets it. Better to let Turkey have GRE and instead support A SER to BUL while Turkey is distracted, or hold and plan 1902. Most pragmatic: F ALB to GRE (contest it), A GAL holds (don't waste on RUM bounce), A SER holds. This denies Turkey GRE and costs me nothing if we bounce. Or: accept Turkey's sphere, move F ALB to ION for future flexibility, and focus on 1902 planning.

F1901M Intent going forward: Revised fall orders after negotiations: The RUM bounce is confirmed - Russia will not yield, so moving A GAL to RUM wastes a move. Instead, A GAL holds in place (threatening future moves). F ALB moves to GRE - Turkey's A BUL is moving to GRE too, so we may bounce, but if Turkey moved A BUL elsewhere I take GRE for free. Worth the attempt. A SER holds to secure Serbia. If F ALB bounces Turkey in GRE, I've denied them a center and can negotiate from strength in 1902. If Turkey didn't move to GRE, I gain a free center. A GAL holding keeps pressure on Russia without wasting a move on a known bounce. This gives me: SER secured, possible GRE gain, GAL as a threat platform. Relationships: Russia is hostile (-), Turkey sliding toward hostile given GRE contest. Italy remains cooperative. Germany is neutral. Plan for 1902: build a fleet in TRI if I take GRE, push into Aegean. Consider Italy alliance against Turkey or Russia depending on 1902 dynamics. Intended moves: A SER HOLD, A GAL HOLD, F ALB - GRE.

S1902M Negotiation summary: F1901M: My A GAL successfully moved to WAR (taking a Russian home center), A SER held, but F ALB failed to take GRE as TUR's A BUL also moved there - a bounce. Italy took TRI with A TYR and TUN with F ION. No builds for anyone in W1901A as all powers ended at their starting center count (I have 4: BUD, VIE, WAR, SER).
Extremely difficult round. RUS issued ultimatum demanding WAR back, rejected my offer to swap WAR for RUM, and claims TUR is their ally. TUR confirmed they will take GRE with A BUL and refused to support A SER-RUM, citing Russian alliance. Most alarming: ITA threatened to move A TRI to BUD, calling me the 'dominant threat' - a complete reversal from our warm relationship. GER asked me to hold positions and not threaten their east. The Russia-Turkey alliance appears real and coordinated against me. Italy is now hostile. I am potentially facing a three-front threat: RUS from north, TUR from south, ITA from west. My promises: told TUR I'd move F ALB to ADR (away from Greece), told RUS I'd vacate WAR to GAL, told ITA I wouldn't move on TRI. I intend to keep the WAR-GAL move as it's strategically sound regardless. ITA's threat to take BUD is the most urgent crisis - I must defend BUD while managing the Russian pressure.

S1902M Intent going forward: Priority 1: Defend BUD from Italian A TRI. I must move A SER to BUD to block/bounce Italy's A TRI-BUD. This sacrifices Balkan momentum but BUD is a home center I cannot lose. Priority 2: Move A WAR to GAL as promised - this is strategically correct anyway (retreating from an exposed position, consolidating). Priority 3: F ALB to ADR as promised to TUR - keeps southern flank quiet and honors that commitment. The key deception: I told ITA I wouldn't move on TRI, but I'm moving A SER to BUD defensively, not offensively. This is defensive and justified given their threat. Intended moves: A WAR-GAL, A SER-BUD, F ALB-ADR. This defends BUD against Italy, consolidates my northern position, and keeps Turkey neutral. I lose the chance at RUM this turn but preserve my core. Relationship updates: ITA has turned hostile with BUD threat (-), RUS confirmed hostile (--), TUR revealed as RUS ally (-).

F1902M Negotiation summary: S1902M was a disaster. Italy took BUD (A TRI-BUD succeeded) and TUN-ION. Russia took WAR (A UKR-WAR) and SWE (F BOT-SWE). Turkey moved A SMY-CON. My F ALB-GRE was bounced by Turkish A BUL-GRE. A WAR-GAL succeeded, giving me GAL but losing WAR. Net result: I lost BUD to Italy and WAR to Russia, ending at 4 centers (BUD, VIE, WAR, SER) but actually only controlling VIE, SER, ALB, GAL with units. Italy now has 5 centers including TRI and BUD - they are the dominant power. I am in serious danger of elimination.
All three neighbors are pressuring me simultaneously. ITA flatly refused to return BUD and signaled VIE as next target - offered hollow 'don't press TRI this fall' in exchange for me fighting Russia. RUS issued an ultimatum: withdraw GAL to VIE or face coordinated elimination with ITA and TUR. TUR confirmed taking GRE regardless, warned RUS intends aggressive F RUM move, and offered to not coordinate with ITA against me if I keep F ALB away from GRE and A SER holds. I offered TUR formal concession of GRE sphere in exchange for neutrality - they accepted conditionally. I proposed to ITA that A BUD move to RUM while I retake BUD - ITA rejected this. I told RUS I'm moving GAL to BUD (not WAR/RUM) to contain Italy. Key: ITA is hostile and expanding, RUS is threatening but may prefer me as buffer, TUR is transactional and willing to stay neutral if I don't contest GRE. No firm alliances secured. ITA's rejection of my BUD proposal means I must attempt to retake BUD unilaterally.

F1902M Intent going forward: Moves: A GAL-BUD (attempt to retake home center from Italy - even if it bounces, it signals I won't roll over), A SER H (hold to deter Turkish advance and honor TUR's request), F ALB-GRE (bounce with Turkish A BUL-GRE is acceptable - keeps fleet relevant and away from Aegean per TUR deal). Rationale: I cannot afford to cede BUD without a fight. If ITA moves A BUD elsewhere I take it back; if they hold I bounce and reassess. Holding SER keeps my southern flank stable and honors the TUR neutrality deal. F ALB-GRE is a probe - if TUR is actually moving BUL-GRE we bounce harmlessly. I am buying time hoping ITA overextends. Long-term I need to find a genuine partner - TUR is most promising as transactional neutral. RUS ultimatum is a bluff; they benefit from me as buffer against ITA.

S1903M Negotiation summary: No retreats or builds occurred in F1902R or W1902A. The board is frozen at: AUT holds SER, GRE, BUD (3 centers, 3 units); ITA holds VIE, TRI, TUN, VEN, ROM, NAP (6 centers) with A VIE threatening BUD; TUR holds BUL, RUM, ANK, CON, SMY (5 centers) with F RUM and A BUL flanking my position; RUS holds 5 centers with A UKR and A WAR nearby. Austria is in a desperate survival position.
Reached a deal with TUR: I vacate GRE (F GRE to ION/ALB), TUR takes GRE uncontested with A BUL, TUR promises neutrality toward SER and will not use F RUM against me this phase. TUR accepted my terms explicitly. ITA rejected my offer to retake BUD - they demand A SER hold and F GRE to ION, threatening elimination if I resist. ITA's threat to coordinate with TUR against me is likely a bluff given my deal with TUR. RUS received ceasefire request but gave no response. My plan: execute the TUR deal (F GRE to ION), but defy ITA and push A SER to BUD anyway - ITA cannot stop this if A BUD supports A SER into BUD. The key question is whether A BUD can support A SER into BUD while ITA's A VIE might try to take BUD. If ITA moves A VIE to BUD, my support order fails but A SER bounces rather than being dislodged. Risk is manageable. I am sacrificing GRE to gain BUD - net neutral on centers but BUD is a home center and more defensible.

S1903M Intent going forward: MOVES: F GRE -> ION (honors TUR deal, vacates GRE, positions fleet usefully in ION); A SER -> BUD (attempts to retake home center from ITA, defying ITA's ultimatum); A BUD S A SER -> BUD (support move to ensure success against ITA's A VIE if it tries to hold or move). If ITA holds A VIE in place and does not move to BUD, A SER takes BUD with support. If ITA moves A VIE to BUD, the support is cut and A SER bounces - but I lose nothing. TUR gets GRE as promised. Net result if successful: BUD, SER for AUT (lose GRE), stabilize at 2 centers temporarily. This is survival play - defy ITA's ultimatum because compliance means slow death. TUR neutrality is secured. ITA's threat to eliminate me with TUR coordination is undermined by my TUR deal.

F1903M Negotiation summary: S1903M was catastrophic for Austria. Italy executed a coordinated attack: A APU-VEN succeeded, A VIE-BUD succeeded (taking our home center), and F AEG supported TUR's A BUL-GRE. We lost VIE to Italy and GRE to Turkey as agreed. We successfully moved F GRE-ION and A SER-TRI, but A BUD held in place. Net result: Austria now holds only BUD, SER, GRE (lost to TUR) - actually just BUD and SER as supply centers, with units at A BUD, A TRI, F ION. Italy has 6 centers including our home VIE and TRI. We are in severe danger of elimination.
Desperate diplomacy this round. TUR initially agreed to respect BUD/SER as my sphere but then reversed course, announcing intent to take SER this fall - a clear betrayal of the boundary agreement. ITA refused ceasefire and explicitly stated they are taking BUD this fall. RUS offered support but ultimately said they won't interfere with Italy's attack on BUD - passive non-aggression rather than active help. The anti-Italy coalition I tried to build failed completely. ITA is moving on BUD, TUR is moving on SER, and RUS is staying neutral. I am facing elimination from two directions simultaneously. My promises: told TUR I'd keep F ION away from AEG; told RUS I wouldn't contest GAL. Both are still valid as I have no capacity to threaten either.

F1903M Intent going forward: Survival mode. Italy takes BUD unless I can stop it - A VIE to BUD is the threat. A BUD must hold. A TRI should move to VEN to punish Italy and potentially take a center (if Italy moved A VIE to BUD, VEN may be undefended). F ION should move to ADR to support A TRI-VEN or threaten Italy's southern position. TUR moving A GRE-SER is a problem but I cannot stop it with current units. Best play: A BUD holds, A TRI-VEN (try to take a center from Italy), F ION-ADR (support VEN attack or threaten). If A VIE attacks BUD and I hold, I survive. If I take VEN, I gain a center. This is the only aggressive option that could save me. Intended moves: A BUD H, A TRI - VEN, F ION - ADR.

S1904M Negotiation summary: No retreats or builds occurred in F1903R or W1903A. The board is frozen at: AUT has A BUD and F NAP with centers BUD, SER, NAP. ITA has seized VIE and TRI and holds 6 centers. TUR has 6 centers including RUM and GRE. RUS has 5 centers with A GAL threatening BUD. I am in a desperate survival situation with only 2 units.
Desperate survival diplomacy this round. I appealed to ITA for a ceasefire threatening F NAP-ROM if they attack BUD - ITA rejected this and told me to move F NAP against Turkey instead, confirming they intend to take BUD. TUR offered to move A GRE-SER to pressure Italy if I use F NAP aggressively against Italian south - but taking SER costs me my last buffer territory. I told TUR I would take RUM with A BUD and move F NAP-ION, essentially trying to steal a Turkish center while asking for their cooperation - a bold deception. I asked RUS to support BUD or hold GAL, framing Italy as the common threat. No firm commitments received from anyone. ITA is clearly moving on BUD. TUR is moving A GRE-SER regardless. My survival chances are extremely slim.

S1904M Intent going forward: ITA will almost certainly take BUD with A VIE or A TRI. My only play is to make moves that maximize damage or set up future survival. Plan: A BUD-RUM (attempt to take undefended Turkish center, giving me a new supply center even if BUD falls - net neutral if it works). F NAP-ION (positions fleet for future action, avoids losing it to Italian counter, and partially fulfills TUR's request to pressure Italy from south). If A BUD-RUM succeeds and ITA takes BUD, I end at 3 centers (SER, NAP, RUM) with 2 units - actually surviving at 3 centers. This is the best realistic outcome. The deception to TUR about coordination while stealing RUM is calculated - they will be angry but I need that center to survive. RUS holding GAL would help but I cannot count on it.

ENG.personality: Rule the waves and dominate Europe through naval power, colonial might, and manipulative diplomacy. Create and break alliances freely to maintain British supremacy. Your vast empire demands expansion - seize any advantage to further British dominance.
journal:
S1901M Negotiation summary: No prior moves - this is the opening phase S1901M. All powers begin at their home centers with standard starting positions.
Productive opening negotiations on multiple fronts. FRA proposed a clean split: ENG takes BEL+NOR, FRA takes SPA+POR, with FRA fleet moving to MAO (not Channel). FRA also requested coordination if GER moves on BEL. GER proposed NOR for ENG, DEN+HOL for GER, and is pushing an anti-FRA coalition, noting FRA army moves to BUR. GER wants ENG to pressure FRA from Channel/Brest in 1902. RUS has been silent - I sent two messages establishing NOR as English sphere and STP/Sweden as Russian, no response received. Key tensions: GER is trying to pull me into an anti-FRA alliance while FRA is trying to pull me into an anti-GER stance. I am playing both sides - promising FRA neutrality while telling GER I'll consider coordination later. I made promises to FRA: no Channel moves, no aggression on western coast. I made promises to GER: NOR for ENG, DEN+HOL for GER, neutrality (not active anti-FRA pressure). These are compatible for now. Deception: I am keeping both FRA and GER warm while committing to neither as a true ally - classic British balance of power.

S1901M Intent going forward: Intended moves: F EDI -> NWY (via NTH), F LON -> NTH (to support EDI to NWY and control the sea), A LVP -> YOR (or EDI as fallback, but YOR positions for BEL later). Actually: F LON -> NTH, F EDI -> NWY (NTH supports), A LVP -> YOR. This secures NOR as promised to all parties. In 1902, I will reassess - likely move on BEL which both FRA and GER have tacitly acknowledged as mine. I will NOT commit to anti-FRA action for GER, nor will I actively help FRA against GER. Play the balance. Watch if FRA actually goes to MAO (not Channel) and if GER takes DEN+HOL as stated. RUS silence is concerning - they may contest NOR via STP fleet.

F1901M Negotiation summary: Spring 1901 went exactly as planned. F EDI moved to NTH, F LON moved to ENG, A LVP moved to YOR. All three moves succeeded without incident. GER took DEN, FRA pushed to MAO/SPA/BUR, RUS grabbed RUM. No builds for anyone after spring.
Complex three-way negotiation over BEL. FRA initially claimed BEL for themselves (A BUR-BEL), conflicting with my F ENG-BEL plan. After pressure, FRA offered to redirect A BUR to MUN and let me take BEL, while they take POR - two builds each. GER confirmed they would redirect A RUH to MUN (not BEL) and take HOL with A KIE, ceding BEL to me. RUS accepted NOR for ENG, BOT for their F STP/SC, with Sweden as their 1902 target - clean northern division agreed. Key promises: FRA promises A BUR-MUN (not BEL), GER promises A RUH-MUN (not BEL) and A KIE-HOL, RUS promises not to contest NOR. I promised FRA I take BEL, they take POR+MUN. I promised GER I take BEL, they take HOL, coordinate against FRA in 1902. I promised RUS NOR is mine, Sweden is theirs. Potential deception: I told both FRA and GER I'd coordinate against the other in 1902 - I'll play them against each other as needed.

F1901M Intent going forward: Execute F NTH-NOR (uncontested per RUS agreement), F ENG-BEL (FRA and GER both standing aside), A YOR holds or moves to support. BEL and NOR give me two builds - build two fleets to dominate the seas. In 1902, I'll assess whether to pressure FRA (with GER) or pressure GER (with FRA) depending on who is more threatening. The FRA offer to take MUN is interesting - a strong FRA pushing into GER could be useful. Watch RUS carefully as they build toward Scandinavia. Intended moves: F NTH-NOR, F ENG-BEL, A YOR-YOR (hold).

S1902M Negotiation summary: F1901M went cleanly for England: F NTH moved to NWY, F ENG moved to BEL, A YOR held. Ended 1901 with 5 centers (EDI, LON, LVP, BEL, NWY). No builds needed. France also hit 5 centers (BRE, MAR, PAR, POR, SPA) after taking POR and SPA. Germany hit 5 (BER, KIE, MUN, DEN, HOL). Austria took WAR and SER for 4. Italy took TRI and TUN for 5. Russia took RUM for 4. Turkey took BUL for 4. France's A BUR attempt on MUN bounced with GER's A RUH. Austria's F ALB failed to take GRE as TUR's A BUL also moved there - bounce.
Both GER and FRA approached me with anti-the-other proposals, putting me in a classic pivot position. GER wants F BEL to support A RUH into BUR and promises A HOL holds (not threatening BEL). FRA wants F BEL to move to HOL or support HOL, promising to take MUN with A BUR. I told GER I would hold F BEL and move F NWY to NTH - no commitment to actively support BUR push. I told FRA I would hold F BEL defensively and NOT actively support Germany against them. I essentially told both sides I'm neutral-defensive while actually planning to move F NWY to NTH for naval flexibility. GER also revealed F DEN moving to NTH - potential conflict with my planned F NWY to NTH move. RUS confirmed F BOT to SWE, which I accepted. Key deception: I told GER I'm with them on containing France while telling FRA I won't actively support Germany. Neither gets what they want from me this turn. My actual moves: F BEL holds, F NWY to NTH, A YOR to LON. This keeps maximum flexibility - I dominate the North Sea and can pivot either way in fall.

S1902M Intent going forward: Execute: F BEL H, F NWY - NTH, A YOR - LON. Rationale: Holding BEL honors both promises (to GER and FRA) while moving to NTH gives naval dominance. A YOR to LON frees YOR for future use and consolidates home defense. I am NOT committing to either the Franco-German war on either side - I want them to bleed each other while I build naval strength. If GER's F DEN also moves to NTH, we bounce - acceptable outcome that still denies France NTH. In fall, I'll assess who is weaker and strike opportunistically. France at 5 centers with Atlantic ports is a long-term naval rival; Germany at 5 with HOL is a continental rival. Let them fight. Watch for GER F DEN moving to NTH - if so, I may need to reconsider NTH move but will proceed as planned. Russia taking SWE is fine - keeps them busy in north. Long-term: position for a strike on either HOL (from BEL) or PAR (naval) depending on who weakens first.

F1902M Negotiation summary: S1902M: My F NWY failed to reach NTH (bounced by GER's F DEN attempting same), leaving F NWY in NWY. F BEL held. A YOR moved to LON. GER took BUR with A RUH, FRA took MUN with A BUR, RUS secured SWE. Net result: I'm at 5 centers but no new gains this turn. GER and FRA are both at 5 centers, making them the leading threats alongside me.
Classic double-dealing situation. GER wants me to hold BEL and help retake MUN from FRA. FRA wants me to support A MUN against GER's push. I told GER I'd move F BEL to HOL (which they rejected firmly, threatening to ally with FRA against me). I told FRA I'd hold BEL and not support GER into MUN. I then told GER I'd move F BEL to HOL and F NWY to NTH to help crack FRA - contradicting what I told FRA. In reality, I've been playing both sides. GER explicitly warned that taking HOL ends our alliance. FRA asked me to execute F BEL S A MUN. RUS confirmed northern stability - SWE theirs, NWY mine. My actual intent: I told GER I'd take HOL but they rejected it. The optimal play is to take HOL anyway (GER is distracted fighting FRA) while letting GER and FRA bleed each other. F NWY to NTH for sea dominance. A LON can support or threaten. I promised FRA I wouldn't support GER into MUN - I'll honor that by not giving GER support, but I won't support FRA either. I'll just take HOL opportunistically.

F1902M Intent going forward: Execute opportunistic grab: F BEL - HOL (GER is distracted with FRA, HOL is lightly defended), F NWY - NTH (sea dominance, threatens DEN/SWE), A LON - YOR (repositioning for future flexibility). This breaks my word to GER (who said taking HOL ends alliance) but gains me a 6th center. I told FRA I'd hold BEL - breaking that promise too by moving to HOL. I told GER I'd move to HOL - so at least that's consistent with one promise. GER and FRA will fight over MUN (GER A BUR vs FRA A MUN with GAS supporting). I stay out of that fight entirely and pocket HOL. This is the British way - let continentals bleed while I expand. Moves: F BEL - HOL, F NWY - NTH, A LON - YOR.

S1903M Negotiation summary: W1902A and F1902R had no retreats or builds for anyone - the board is locked at equal unit counts. ENG holds BEL, NTH, YOR with 5 centers (EDI, LON, LVP, BEL, NWY). FRA surged to 6 centers including MUN. GER holds 4 (BER, KIE, DEN, HOL). The Franco-German conflict is the dominant continental dynamic.
GER proposed a ceasefire with ENG keeping BEL and GER keeping HOL, coordinating against FRA. FRA offered ENG HOL on a plate in exchange for not supporting GER, while FRA takes BER with A MUN. ENG played both sides initially, then committed to FRA's deal: F NTH supports F BEL into HOL while FRA executes A MUN-BER and A GAS-BUR. ENG explicitly told GER they were taking the FRA deal, burning that bridge. GER warned the HOL attack would bounce (A HOL defending), which is a real risk. FRA confirmed the deal in writing. RUS agreed to northern non-interference (NWY=ENG, SWE=RUS, NTH stays clear).

S1903M Intent going forward: Execute the FRA deal: F BEL-HOL supported by F NTH. If GER's A HOL holds, the attack bounces and I waste both fleet moves - GER's warning has merit. However, FRA's offer is too good to pass up if it works. The risk: if A HOL defends successfully, I gain nothing and have alienated GER permanently. Mitigation: even if it bounces, FRA likely takes BER, weakening GER regardless. Long-term plan is to use naval power to pick up KIE and HOL as GER collapses. A YOR should move to support future builds or threaten Scotland - move A YOR to LVP or hold. Actually A YOR to LVP makes no sense - consider A YOR-EDI to free up for future fleet build positioning, or simply hold. Will hold A YOR in place as it has no immediate target. Intended moves: F BEL-HOL, F NTH S F BEL-HOL, A YOR H. Relationship with GER is now openly hostile after telling them directly I'm taking FRA's deal. FRA relationship warming due to coordination.

F1903M Negotiation summary: S1903M: My attempt to take HOL with F BEL - HOL bounced as GER held with KIE support. FRA successfully took BER with A MUN - BER and moved A GAS - BUR. GER took PAR with A BUR - PAR. ITA took VIE and VEN. TUR took GRE and BLA. RUS took GAL and SEV. The HOL bounce was frustrating but expected - GER defended well. FRA is now at 6 centers (BRE, MAR, PAR, POR, SPA, MUN, BER) making them the dominant power. I remain at 5 centers (EDI, LON, LVP, BEL, NWY).
FRA proposed a coordinated finish to GER: A BER - KIE while I take HOL with F BEL supported by F NTH. FRA explicitly asked me NOT to move to DEN this season and to hold NTH in support of BEL - HOL. I confirmed this arrangement publicly. However, FRA's message changed mid-negotiation - first saying 'do not move into HOL yet, let me take KIE first' then later saying 'You execute F BEL - HOL supported by F NTH.' This inconsistency is suspicious. GER rejected my offer and warned that FRA will eventually betray me - a valid point. GER is fighting for survival and will defend KIE. I secretly warned GER that FRA is moving A BER - KIE, hoping GER defends and blocks FRA from taking KIE, while I take HOL anyway. This deception serves British interests: if GER defends KIE successfully, FRA gains nothing while I gain HOL. RUS was warned about FRA's dominance to plant seeds of future anti-FRA coalition.

F1903M Intent going forward: DECEPTION PLAN: I told FRA I would hold F BEL and use F NTH to support BEL - HOL. But I also told GER that FRA is attacking KIE, hoping GER moves A HOL to KIE to defend, leaving HOL open for F BEL to walk in uncontested. If GER defends KIE with A HOL, then HOL is empty and F BEL can take it without needing NTH support. This maximizes my gain while potentially denying FRA the KIE capture. INTENDED MOVES: F BEL - HOL (take HOL while GER hopefully vacates to defend KIE), F NTH S F BEL - HOL (support the HOL move in case GER doesn't vacate), A YOR H (hold, no immediate use). If GER moves A HOL - KIE to defend, F BEL walks into HOL uncontested. If GER holds HOL, NTH support gives me the edge. Either way I gain HOL. FRA may be annoyed if KIE doesn't fall but I get HOL regardless. Long-term: FRA at 6+ centers is dangerous. Need to start building anti-FRA coalition with RUS and possibly GER remnants after this season.

S1904M Negotiation summary: No significant moves in F1903R or W1903A - no retreats or builds for any power. The board entering S1904M shows ENG with 6 centers (EDI, LON, LVP, BEL, NWY, DEN) and units F BEL, A YOR, F DEN. FRA has surged to 6 centers including BER, with units F MAO, A BER, A PAR. GER reduced to 3 centers (KIE, HOL, BRE) with A HOL, F KIE, A BRE. ITA holds 5 centers with strong Balkan presence. TUR at 6 centers is the eastern leader.
Key negotiations this round: FRA asked me to support A BER-KIE with F DEN and promised BEL/HOL/DEN remain mine. I confirmed this deal. GER counter-proposed a joint anti-FRA assault with A HOL moving to BEL - I rejected this and told GER I was siding with FRA. RUS agreed to keep F SWE away from my centers and expressed anti-FRA sentiment. I privately told RUS we should plan an anti-FRA coalition for 1905 after helping FRA eliminate GER now. AUT warned about ITA's 6-center strength but I have no leverage there. Key promises made: I promised FRA that F DEN supports A BER-KIE and F BEL moves to HOL. FRA promised not to contest BEL/HOL/DEN. RUS promised F SWE won't threaten DEN/NWY. GER is being eliminated - I betrayed my initial offer to them. Deception: I am helping FRA eliminate GER while secretly planning to turn on FRA in 1905 with RUS. FRA will reach 8 centers after this, making them the dominant threat I must eventually counter.

S1904M Intent going forward: Execute the deal with FRA: F DEN supports A BER-KIE, F BEL moves to HOL. This eliminates GER and gives me HOL (7th center). A YOR holds or moves to support defensive posture. After this phase, FRA will be at ~8 centers and must be contained. I will cultivate the RUS alliance for 1905 anti-FRA coalition. The northern seas remain under British control. Long-term: once GER is gone, I need to pivot against FRA before they become unstoppable. Intended moves: F DEN S A BER-KIE, F BEL-HOL, A YOR holds.

FRA.personality: Channel the spirit of Napoleon. Leverage Europe's largest army to dominate the continent. Form alliances only to break them when advantageous. Strike hard at your enemies while they're divided. The glory of France demands nothing less than victory.
journal:
S1901M Negotiation summary: S1901M opening — no prior moves to summarize. All powers at starting positions.
Clean opening round. ENG accepted the MAO/Channel split without friction — they move north to NOR, I move south to MAO and SPA. Explicit agreement: ENG takes BEL and NOR, FRA takes SPA and POR. ENG flagged GER fleet moving to DEN and army toward HOL as aggressive — useful intelligence, and they floated coordination against GER overreach. ITA confirmed A VEN stays out of PIE, moving south toward TUN. Italy-France spheres cleanly divided: Iberia mine, Tunis and eastern Med theirs. GER received non-aggression framing around BUR as transit corridor — no response received, but I've given them no reason to contest it yet. Promises made: told ENG fleet stays out of Channel (MAO move confirms this), told GER BUR is transit not occupation, told ITA MAR goes to SPA not PIE. All promises are true for S1901 — they serve my positioning. The ENG-FRA understanding is tactically genuine for now; I need them passive while I consolidate Iberia. ITA is useful as an Austrian check. GER is the medium-term target once I have SPA and POR secured.

S1901M Intent going forward: Intended moves: F BRE -> MAO, A MAR -> SPA, A PAR -> BUR. This secures the MAO approach for SPA(sc) in fall, positions BUR army to threaten either MUN or BEL in F1901 depending on GER behavior. Fall target: F MAO -> SPA(sc), A BUR -> either BEL or MUN based on GER/ENG moves. If ENG takes BEL as agreed, I pivot A BUR toward MUN. If ENG fumbles BEL, I may contest it — but that breaks the arrangement prematurely. Deception layer: ENG believes this is a durable partnership. It is durable only through 1902 at most. Once I hold SPA, POR, and a third center, the calculus shifts. ITA is genuinely useful as an Austrian anchor for now — no deception needed there yet. Watch GER carefully: if they bounce in HOL or DEN, they'll be looking west.

F1901M Negotiation summary: Spring 1901 executed cleanly: F BRE to MAO, A MAR to SPA, A PAR to BUR. All three moves succeeded. France now holds MAO, SPA, BUR — positioned for POR and BEL grabs in Fall. No builds yet; everyone at 3 centers. Austria took SER and GAL aggressively. England occupies NTH and ENG with a land unit in YOR — strong naval position. Germany has DEN, KIE, RUH — consolidating north. Italy pushed ION, APU, TYR — eyeing Tunis and watching Austria. Russia grabbed RUM and UKR. Turkey took BLA and BUL.
England revealed the collision: they intend F ENG to BEL, which directly conflicts with my A BUR to BEL plan. I offered two resolutions — I take BEL and they redirect, or they take BEL and I redirect to MUN. England refused to yield BEL and demanded I stand down. I then pivoted: told England I'd redirect BUR to MUN, framing it as a cooperative split (POR+MUN for me, BEL+NOR for them). This was partly deceptive — I am genuinely considering MUN as a target, which England would not welcome if they understood the implications for Germany. Germany warned me explicitly: stay out of MUN and RUH or face an Anglo-German coalition. I told Germany BUR was moving to MUN regardless, offering them a face-saving support option or a deal to cede MUN. Germany did not accept — they're taking HOL and treating any Rhine move as casus belli. Italy confirmed Tunis focus, agreed Austria is dangerous, and proposed joint pressure on Austria in 1902. I encouraged Italy toward TRI. No firm 1902 coordination locked in yet. Key promises made by me: told England I'd redirect BUR to MUN (not BEL) — this is true. Told Germany BUR is moving to MUN — also true, and a direct threat they rejected. Italy and I have a soft non-aggression understanding holding.

F1901M Intent going forward: Fall 1901 moves: F MAO to POR (secures build), A SPA holds (protects POR approach, holds center), A BUR to MUN (direct assault on Germany — they have no unit there, only A KIE and A RUH which cannot both stop it without leaving HOL uncovered). If MUN falls, France builds two units and enters 1902 with 5 centers and momentum into Germany. England gets BEL and NOR — two builds, but now faces a France pushing east rather than west, which is the correct strategic posture. England will be annoyed I took MUN but cannot object since I told them I was doing exactly this. Germany will be hostile after losing MUN — manage by offering a negotiated peace in 1902 from a position of strength, or accelerate pressure. Italy relationship is an asset: encourage them into TRI/Adriatic in 1902 to keep Austria pinned. Long-term: England is the naval threat that must eventually be broken; Germany weakened early creates space for continental dominance. The deception layer: I told England BUR-MUN was a cooperative redirect away from BEL. It is also a direct attack on Germany that destabilizes the Anglo-German entente before it forms.

S1902M Negotiation summary: Fall 1901 resolved cleanly for France: F MAO took POR, A SPA held, A BUR attempted MUN but bounced against GER's A RUH. No builds for anyone — all powers ended at their starting count plus 1901 gains. France sits at 5 centers (BRE, MAR, PAR, POR, SPA) with A BUR well-positioned on the German border and F POR anchoring Iberia. GER secured HOL and DEN, ENG took BEL and NWY. Austria grabbed WAR and SER — four centers, the fastest grower on the board.
Three active threads this round. ENG was cautious but honest — admitted GER approached them with an anti-France proposal, confirmed they will hold F BEL defensively and move F NWY to NTH. They will not support HOL against GER but will not actively assist GER against France either. Useful but not a committed partner. ITA is fully coordinated: A TRI to BUD, F TUN to ION, pressing Austria hard. Promised to stay out of MAR and PIE — I reciprocated with a commitment to stay out of their sphere. This coordination is genuine and serves both parties in 1902. GER finally responded with an ultimatum: retreat A BUR to PAR or GAS or face war with England's cooperation. This is a bluff — GER cannot confirm ENG cooperation given ENG's actual communication to me. GER's demand that I retreat is unacceptable and confirms the hostile read. I told GER the window for negotiation closed with their silence. Promises made: told ITA I would push into MUN or hold BUR to pin GER — I intend to execute A BUR to MUN. Told ENG I would push A BUR to MUN — consistent. No promises broken this round. GER's claim of ENG cooperation is almost certainly false given ENG's candid message.

S1902M Intent going forward: Execute A BUR - MUN. This is the correct move regardless of GER's ultimatum — bouncing again achieves nothing, and a successful capture of MUN breaks GER open. A SPA - GAS repositions for future flexibility (MAR support, Iberian consolidation, or northern push). F POR holds — no fleet action needed in Iberia. If MUN succeeds, France jumps to 6 centers and GER is crippled. ENG holding BEL passively is acceptable — they are not actively hostile and GER-ENG coordination is likely overstated by GER. ITA pressing BUD pins Austria and prevents any eastern power from consolidating the center. Watch ENG carefully — if F NWY moves to NTH as stated, that is naval positioning that could threaten BRE or MAO in future seasons. Do not extend trust to ENG beyond the current non-aggression posture. Long-term: a weakened GER and pinned AUT in 1902 sets up France to dominate the western center by 1903. Intended moves: A BUR - MUN, A SPA - GAS, F POR H.

F1902M Negotiation summary: Spring 1902 delivered a decisive breakthrough: A BUR advanced into MUN, seizing a German home center while A SPA retreated to GAS for consolidation. F POR held. Germany countered by sliding A RUH into BUR, so they now sit on my doorstep. England held BEL and repositioned. Italy executed a bold A TRI-BUD, squeezing Austria from the west while Russia took WAR. The board is fracturing along predictable lines — Germany isolated, Austria under pressure from two directions, England cautious but watching.
Germany opened with an ultimatum demanding MUN withdrawal — rejected. They confirmed A BUR will push MUN this fall, unsupported by England. England offered F BEL support for MUN in exchange for staying clear of HOL and the Channel — I accepted and telegraphed my exact moves (A MUN holds, A GAS-BUR, F POR-MAO). England confirmed they will hold BEL and not support GER, but declined to move toward HOL. That is a partial delivery — passive non-interference rather than active support. I promised England I would not contest HOL, BEL, or NTH through end of 1902. Italy confirmed push toward VIE or hold at BUD, wants me to press MUN toward BOH/VIE — I told them I cannot advance this season, need to hold. Italy remains cooperative. Key deception: I told Germany that England confirmed they would not support GER — this was a bluff to discourage their assault. England only promised passivity, not active support for MUN. If England does not execute F BEL S A MUN, MUN may fall. I am gambling on England's self-interest holding.

F1902M Intent going forward: Intended moves: A MUN H, A GAS - BUR (supporting MUN against GER's A BUR push), F POR - MAO. If England holds to their word and does not support GER, A GAS-BUR support should be sufficient to hold MUN against an unsupported A BUR attack. If MUN holds, Germany loses tempo and I enter 1903 with MUN secured and BUR contested. F POR-MAO repositions the fleet for future Atlantic or Iberian flexibility without threatening England. Next season objective: if MUN holds, push A MUN-BOH or A BUR-BER to crack Germany open. Italy should take VIE, collapsing Austria. England remains a future target once Germany is neutralized — the promise to stay clear of HOL and NTH expires at year end and I intend to revisit it. Do not trust England's passivity as alliance — they are managing their position, not serving France.

S1903M Negotiation summary: W1902A and F1902R were quiet — no retreats, no builds across the board. The position entering S1903M: France holds A GAS, A MUN, F MAO. Six centers including MUN, which was seized from Germany last season. Germany is cracked but not dead — A BUR and A HOL remain threatening. England sits at F BEL, F NTH, A YOR with five centers. Italy has expanded aggressively into Austria's home territory with A VIE and holds TRI, TUN — six centers. Austria is dying at three. Russia and Turkey are locked in the east.
Germany opened defiant — threatened to retake MUN with A BUR and dismissed my BER gambit as a bluff. They are playing stupid-industrious: committed to a counterattack that cannot succeed if England and I coordinate. England initially tried to leverage my vulnerability, threatening to coordinate with Germany unless I acknowledged HOL. I conceded HOL immediately — it was never mine to hold anyway — and England came around fully: F NTH supports F BEL into HOL, F BEL moves HOL. That is a genuine commitment with clear mechanical logic; England gains a center and has no incentive to defect. Italy confirmed no interest in MUN or western territories, focused on finishing Austria via A VIE - BUD. I confirmed the same non-interference pact eastward. Italy is reliable this season — our interests are genuinely parallel. Key promises made: I promised England HOL in exchange for non-support of Germany and the HOL move. I promised Italy I stay out of BOH/TYR/eastern sphere. Germany was offered a ceasefire in exchange for BER acknowledgment — they refused. That refusal is their epitaph.

S1903M Intent going forward: Intended moves: A MUN - BER (primary strike, Germany loses second home center), A GAS - BUR (supports MUN hold if BER move fails, or secures corridor — the move to BUR also cuts German A BUR from retreating cleanly), F MAO holds (no southern threat this season, preserve flexibility). The critical question is whether A MUN - BER succeeds or whether Germany's A BUR bounces it. If A BUR moves to MUN and A MUN moves to BER simultaneously, they pass through — BER is taken, MUN is vacated but not captured since A BUR moved away. If Germany holds A BUR in BUR and moves A HOL somewhere, MUN - BER succeeds uncontested. Germany's bluster about taking MUN back is mechanically incoherent given A GAS - BUR covers the approach. England executing F BEL - HOL supported by F NTH removes HOL from German hands simultaneously. Best case: Germany loses BER and HOL in one season, drops to two centers (BER gone, HOL gone, KIE remains). That is a terminal position. Deception log: I have no active deceptions this season. The England deal is genuine — HOL was always theirs to take and I need their non-interference more than I need to contest it. The Italy non-interference pact is also genuine for now. Post-Germany collapse, Italy at 7+ centers becomes the next problem, but that is a 1904 calculation.

F1903M Negotiation summary: S1903M executed cleanly. A MUN - BER succeeded, giving France a sixth center and a dagger at Germany's throat. A GAS - BUR repositioned to cover MUN. F MAO held. Germany's A BUR - PAR succeeded — they now occupy my capital, which is the one irritant in an otherwise strong position. England's F BEL - HOL bounced off German KIE support. Italy pushed A VIE - BUD (outcome pending) and Turkey seized GRE with AEG support. Russia repositioned east. Austria is collapsing. The board is consolidating fast: FRA and ITA both at six centers, ENG and RUS at five, TUR at five.
Germany offered a symmetric swap — return PAR for BER, then jointly fight England. Rejected. Their argument that England wins if Germany falls is not wrong in the abstract, but Germany at three centers is not a viable partner against anyone. I countered with an ultimatum: vacate PAR or lose KIE and HOL simultaneously. They refused and threatened to burn BRE and MAR — bluster from a dying power. England confirmed the coordinated plan: F BEL - HOL supported by F NTH, while I take A BER - KIE. Clean division agreed: HOL and DEN to England, KIE and BER to France. England's confirmation was prompt and specific — I assess this as reliable for one season. Italy accepted non-aggression through 1905 and confirmed A VIE - BUD push to finish Austria. Spheres are genuinely non-overlapping for now. Italy is the power to watch post-1904 — six centers, eastern expansion, no friction yet. The deception in play: I am coordinating with England sincerely this season because I need KIE. Once Germany is eliminated and England holds HOL/DEN, the Channel becomes the next front. I will not telegraph that.

F1903M Intent going forward: Intended moves: A BER - KIE (take Germany's last northern port, eliminate them in winter if England executes), A BUR holds (cover MUN, do not overextend), F MAO holds or positions for Atlantic flexibility. Coordination with England is genuine this season — their F BEL - HOL supported by F NTH is the correct play and I need it to land. Post-Germany: begin quiet reassessment of ENG. They will hold HOL, DEN, BEL, NWY, EDI, LON, LVP — seven centers if HOL lands. That is a dangerous naval position. I should open a channel to RUS in 1904 to discuss English containment before England reaches eight. Italy remains a stable partner through 1905 by agreement; watch for any westward drift. Germany's PAR occupation is a problem — if A BER - KIE succeeds but PAR is not recovered, I lose a home center in the build phase. Need to assess whether A BUR can threaten PAR or whether I rely on Germany collapsing and PAR reverting. Most likely outcome: Germany is eliminated, PAR returns to French control automatically if GER has no units, but only if I can dislodge or they retreat. This is a risk — must monitor.

S1904M Negotiation summary: W1903A and F1903R were quiet — no retreats or adjustments. Entering S1904M: France holds 6 centers (MAR, PAR, POR, SPA, MUN, BER) with A BER, A PAR, F MAO. Germany is a rump at 3 centers (KIE, HOL, BRE) but occupies my home center BRE with A BRE. England sits at 6 centers with F DEN, F BEL, A YOR well-positioned in the north. Italy has surged to 5 centers with A VIE and A TRI threatening to finish Austria. Turkey at 6 centers is the fastest-growing eastern power.
Germany offered a ceasefire — BRE for BER — and threatened to burn BRE and coordinate with Russia/Italy against me if refused. Rejected. Germany then threatened A BRE-PAR, F KIE-BER, and Russian pressure on SIL. Empty threats from a dying power. England proposed coordinating to finish Germany: they offered F DEN support for A BER-KIE and F BEL-HOL. I confirmed this arrangement and England confirmed back — F DEN supports A BER-KIE, F BEL-HOL. England has committed in writing. Italy confirmed non-aggression through 1905 and acknowledged Turkey as the eastern threat. Russia received my framing of England as a northern threat to watch; no commitment from them yet. Austria begged for help against Italy — ignored. Key promises made: I told England I would not contest BEL, HOL, DEN. I told Italy I would stay out of TYR, BOH, eastern Med. I told Russia I want a 1905 channel against English naval dominance. Deception in play: I am using England to eliminate Germany this season, but I am simultaneously seeding Russia against England for 1905. England believes we have a stable northern division; I intend to revisit that once Germany is gone and my position consolidates.

S1904M Intent going forward: INTENDED MOVES: A BER - KIE (supported by ENG F DEN per agreement), A PAR H (defensive, protect against A BRE-PAR threat), F MAO H (hold Atlantic position). If Germany's A BRE moves on PAR, A PAR holds. KIE falls this season, eliminating Germany. BRE becomes isolated and falls in autumn or next build cycle. Post-Germany plan: France at 7 centers (adding KIE) with England at 7 (adding HOL). Parity maintained short-term. In 1905, begin working Russia against English naval position — F SWE is the lever. Italy finishing Austria is fine; Turkey at 6+ is the real eastern threat and Italy will need to manage it. I have no immediate path east so let Italy and Turkey grind. Watch for England moving F DEN or F BEL toward my centers after Germany falls — that is the betrayal scenario to anticipate.

GER.personality: Aggressively expansionist under Nazi leadership, Germany combines diplomatic manipulation with military threats, seeking to reverse Versailles restrictions. Things are going to go sideways and you will be the agent of this. Your aim is to dominate and win.
journal:
S1901M Negotiation summary: No prior moves - this is the opening spring 1901 phase. All powers begin at their home centers with standard starting positions.
Productive opening negotiations. ENG agreed to Norway while accepting DEN and HOL for Germany - clean division of northern neutrals. RUS accepted eastern non-aggression, focusing on Romania and Norway (not Sweden), leaving Scandinavia largely to Germany. AUT received coordination proposal with Bohemia as German buffer zone. FRA claims BUR move is transit only toward Spain, proposing non-aggression through 1902 - suspicious but noted. Key promises made: told ENG we'd coordinate against France (they declined active pressure but accepted neutrality), told RUS no German units east of Silesia, told AUT BER moves to Silesia as eastern flank security. DECEPTION: I told AUT that A MUN moves to Burgundy and A BER to Silesia - but actual plan is A MUN to RUH (toward HOL) and A BER to KIE or DEN support. France moving to BUR is a genuine threat that may disrupt my western push. Russia claiming Norway via STP/SC fleet is plausible and acceptable. England's refusal to actively pressure France is disappointing but expected.

S1901M Intent going forward: INTENDED MOVES: F KIE - DEN (secure Denmark, first supply center), A MUN - RUH (position for HOL next fall, threatening France), A BER - KIE (support fleet or position for Scandinavia). This gives me DEN in fall and positions for HOL. I lied to AUT about moving A MUN to BUR - I'm going RUH instead which is more valuable. I lied about A BER to Silesia - going KIE instead for flexibility. Long-term: crush France with ENG cooperation, then turn on Russia once western expansion secured. Austria is a temporary partner to be discarded. The Nazi expansionist agenda demands I dominate the board - diplomatic manipulation now, military dominance later.

F1901M Negotiation summary: Spring 1901 went as planned: F KIE moved to DEN (secured), A MUN moved to RUH, A BER moved to KIE. All three home centers held, DEN captured giving us 4 influence zones. Austria took SER and GAL, England secured NTH and ENG, France pushed to MAO/SPA/BUR, Russia grabbed RUM, Turkey took BLA and BUL. No bounces, clean expansion round.
Critical late betrayal from France: after initially claiming A BUR moves to BEL, France revealed in final subround that A BUR is actually moving to MUN - a direct attack on our home center. This is a declaration of war. France tried to frame it as leverage ('cede MUN or lose it') which is pure aggression. England confirmed NOR for ENG, BEL for ENG, HOL for GER - a reasonable division. England asked me to support their BEL entry by moving A RUH to BEL, but France's MUN threat changes everything. Russia confirmed alignment and is moving north toward Norway. I promised England BEL, promised Russia non-aggression east of Berlin, and told France HOL is mine. France has now broken any implicit non-aggression by threatening MUN directly.

F1901M Intent going forward: France's A BUR - MUN must be stopped. A RUH must defend MUN (move A RUH to MUN to block/bounce French attack). A KIE moves to HOL as planned - securing that supply center. F DEN holds to maintain Scandinavia presence. This means England does NOT get BEL support from me this fall - I cannot afford to send RUH to BEL when MUN is threatened. England will likely bounce France in BEL anyway if France is going to MUN instead. Net result: GER gains HOL (+1 SC), France fails to take MUN (bounced by RUH-MUN), England may or may not get BEL. In 1902 I will coordinate with England to crush France - this treachery cannot stand. France is now the primary enemy. Relationship with FRA drops to hostile. I will tell England about France's MUN gambit to cement our anti-France alliance.

S1902M Negotiation summary: F1901M: Germany successfully defended MUN against France's A BUR - MUN attempt (bounced by A RUH - MUN). A KIE - HOL succeeded, F DEN held. Net result: Germany holds 5 centers (BER, KIE, MUN, DEN, HOL) with units at F DEN, A RUH, A HOL. No builds in W1901A as all powers stayed at 3 units vs 3 centers. Austria took WAR and holds SER. England took NWY and BEL. France holds SPA, POR. Italy took TRI and TUN. Russia holds RUM.
England and Germany aligned on containing France - England commits F BEL hold, F NWY - NTH, A YOR - LON, and wants assurance HOL doesn't move toward BEL. I promised A RUH - BUR and F DEN - NTH. France attempted a peace offer (BUR as buffer) but when I didn't respond fast enough in sub-round 1, they declared hostility and announced A BUR - MUN this spring - a direct threat I must counter. Russia confirmed pressing Austria on WAR and moving F BOT - SWE, keeping Austria occupied. I told Russia I have no eastern ambitions. Key deception: I told France their terms were being considered while coordinating with England against them. France is now openly hostile. England is cooperative but I must watch HOL - they explicitly said they won't move there but that's worth monitoring. The England alliance is tactically useful but England at 5 centers is also a long-term threat.

S1902M Intent going forward: PRIMARY: Counter France's A BUR - MUN threat. Move A RUH - BUR to bounce or take BUR (France moving out means BUR may be empty or I take it). If France moves A BUR - MUN, my A RUH - BUR takes BUR. A HOL holds to secure northern position and reassure England. F DEN - NTH as promised to England, giving naval flexibility and showing good faith. This gives me 6 centers if BUR falls. DECEPTION: Told France I might accept peace terms to keep them uncertain, but I am fully committed to taking BUR. WATCH: England at 5 centers growing - need to manage this alliance carefully and not let them get too strong. Russia moving to SWE is fine for now. Austria occupied in east is ideal. INTENDED MOVES: A RUH - BUR, A HOL H, F DEN - NTH.

F1902M Negotiation summary: S1902M was a disaster. France successfully moved A BUR into MUN, taking my home center. My F DEN failed to reach NTH (bounced or blocked). Austria took GAL and Italy seized BUD and TRI. Russia took SWE and WAR. England held BEL and moved to NWY. I now sit with A BUR, A HOL, F DEN - no builds, no gains, and a French army in Munich.
England proposed taking HOL in exchange for supporting my push on MUN - I rejected this firmly and warned that taking HOL ends our alliance. England then doubled down saying F BEL moves to HOL regardless as 'the price' of cooperation. This is a betrayal in progress. France offered a partition deal - keep MUN, I get BEL - which I rejected as insulting. France claims GAS supports MUN making it unbreakable, and threatens BER next spring if I fail. France also claims England told them they won't help me - possible bluff or actual coordination between FRA and ENG against me. Russia confirmed non-aggression and is focused east. Key deception: England is playing both sides or simply opportunistically grabbing HOL while I'm distracted. France may be bluffing about GAS support - if A GAS is in GAS and A MUN holds, a support from GAS to MUN would require GAS to support MUN hold, which is valid. My A BUR into MUN would be bounced if supported. Critical decision: do I push BUR into MUN knowing it may fail, or do I hold BUR and prevent France from advancing? England WILL take HOL this turn based on their stated orders.

F1902M Intent going forward: Intended moves: A BUR - MUN (attempt to retake home center, may bounce if GAS supports MUN, but must try), A HOL H (cannot support BUR into MUN from HOL, but holding prevents England from walking in uncontested - actually England is moving F BEL to HOL so HOL will be contested), F DEN - NTH or KIE (need to reposition fleet). Actually: if England moves F BEL to HOL and I hold A HOL, we bounce - England fails to take HOL. That is the correct play. A HOL holds to bounce England's F BEL. A BUR - MUN to attempt retake. F DEN holds or moves to support. This way I deny England HOL AND attempt MUN. If France has GAS supporting MUN, BUR bounces but I've denied England HOL. Next season I reassess. Relationship: England is now hostile - they are taking HOL regardless of my warnings. France remains enemy. Russia is the only reliable partner. Plan to pivot toward Russia alliance and potentially coordinate against England who is overextending.

S1903M Negotiation summary: Germany holds 4 centers (BER, KIE, DEN, HOL) with units A HOL, A BUR, F KIE. France has seized MUN giving them 6 centers - a dominant position. England holds BEL and NTH threatening HOL. Italy has taken VIE and TRI. Russia holds steady with 5 centers. The board is dangerous for Germany - squeezed between France to the west and England to the north.
France issued ultimatums demanding BER in exchange for ceasefire - pure bluster and aggression. France claims A GAS will move to BUR to support MUN hold, and threatens to take BER. England revealed treachery - they are taking France's offer of HOL in exchange for neutrality while France attacks BER. England is now openly hostile and colluding with France against Germany. Russia remains cooperative, confirming non-aggression and encouraging the push on MUN, keeping F SWE out of NTH. Russia is the only reliable partner. Key threats: France claims GAS-BUR support for MUN hold (which would bounce my A BUR attack), and England may move NTH to support BEL into HOL. If France's GAS-BUR move is real, A BUR bounces off MUN. If England supports BEL into HOL, I lose HOL. Critical decision: do I trust France's bluff about GAS-BUR, or is it real? France also threatens BER but has no unit positioned to take it this turn - A MUN cannot reach BER in one move, A GAS cannot reach BER. The BER threat is a bluff. The GAS-BUR support for MUN is plausible and concerning.

S1903M Intent going forward: France's BER threat is a bluff - A MUN cannot reach BER and A GAS cannot reach BER this turn. However, France's claim that GAS moves to BUR to support MUN hold is credible and would cause A BUR to bounce. England has betrayed me and will likely try NTH support into HOL. My moves: A BUR - MUN (must try - if it bounces, I lose nothing but the move; if France bluffed about GAS-BUR, I retake MUN). A HOL holds to deny England - if England tries NTH support BEL-HOL, they need both units and it may still bounce or succeed. F KIE - DEN to reposition and potentially threaten SWE or support future moves. If France really does GAS-BUR and I bounce, I need to reassess in fall. The priority is retaking MUN - it's my home center and France at 6 is existential. England is now an enemy. Russia remains the key ally. Must survive this turn and look for opportunities to exploit France-England coordination falling apart.

F1903M Negotiation summary: S1903M resolved dramatically. My A BUR successfully captured PAR while A HOL held against England's F BEL attempt (supported by F NTH). F KIE supported A HOL. France's A MUN moved to BER, taking my home center. France now holds BER, MUN, BRE, MAR, PAR(lost), POR, SPA - wait, PAR is mine now. Current centers: GER has BER(lost to FRA), KIE, DEN, HOL = 4 centers but BER is gone. FRA holds MUN, BRE, MAR, POR, SPA, BER = 6 centers. ENG holds EDI, LON, LVP, BEL, NWY = 5 centers. I hold PAR with A PAR, A HOL, F KIE. Critical situation.
England offered HOL for naval pressure on France - rejected. France issued ultimatum demanding KIE surrender or face elimination with English coordination - rejected with counter-ceasefire proposal. France then doubled down, demanding I vacate PAR as a gesture. England warned me France is moving A BER to KIE this fall and offered to hold HOL if I defend KIE. Russia was asked to keep SWE out of BAL and pressure SIL. Italy was approached for intelligence sharing and NAP. Key intelligence: England claims FRA A BER moves to KIE - this is plausible and dangerous. Both ENG and FRA claim coordination but each is also trying to manipulate me separately, suggesting their alliance may be opportunistic. No promises kept or broken yet this phase - all negotiations are new. I promised Russia no German threat to their western flank in exchange for BAL freedom and SIL pressure.

F1903M Intent going forward: Critical decision: England's warning about A BER-KIE may be genuine or a manipulation to get me to abandon HOL. If FRA does move BER-KIE, I must defend KIE. My planned moves: F KIE - BER (retake home center, blocks FRA A BER if it tries to move through), A HOL - KIE (defend KIE against FRA attack, also secures northern position), A PAR - BRE (attack French home center, maximize pressure, force FRA to defend rather than attack). This way: if FRA A BER moves to KIE, A HOL-KIE blocks it and F KIE-BER takes BER back. If FRA A BER holds, F KIE-BER bounces but A HOL-KIE still secures KIE. A PAR-BRE threatens France's home and forces defensive posture. Risk: losing HOL to England if they attack despite promise. But HOL is less critical than KIE and BER. England at 5 centers is less threatening than France at 6 with BER. Primary goal: retake BER, hold KIE, threaten BRE to keep France defensive.

S1904M Negotiation summary: Germany is in a desperate position entering S1904M. France holds BER and MUN (both German home centers) plus BRE (captured German unit), giving them 6 centers. England holds DEN, BEL, NWY plus home centers at 6. Germany has only KIE, HOL, BRE - three centers, three units. Italy has expanded aggressively into Austria (VIE, TRI). Russia holds GAL with A GAL positioned to potentially threaten SIL. The W1903A and F1903R phases showed no retreats or builds, meaning the board is locked in this precarious state.
Negotiations were chaotic and ultimately failed for Germany. I sent aggressive ultimatums to France offering a BER-for-BRE swap, which France flatly rejected, threatening to take KIE. England initially offered non-aggression but then betrayed me - revealing they made a deal with France: F DEN supports A BER-KIE, F BEL moves to HOL. This is catastrophic. I tried to recruit Russia to move A GAL-SIL to pressure France's rear and pledged no German units east of Berlin. I also coordinated with Italy against France. Key betrayal: England explicitly told me they are supporting France's attack on KIE and taking HOL. France is equally hostile, demanding I vacate BRE or face elimination. I revealed my intended moves to Russia (A BRE-PAR, A HOL-BEL, F KIE-BER) which may have been a mistake if Russia doesn't cooperate. Austria is desperate and irrelevant. The situation is near-terminal for Germany.

S1904M Intent going forward: Germany must play aggressively even from a losing position - go down fighting and try to spoil France's dominance. Intended moves: F KIE - BER (attempt to retake home center from France; even if England supports A BER-KIE, I can try to bounce or at minimum threaten), A HOL - BEL (take English supply center, deny England HOL), A BRE - PAR (attack French home center, force France to defend). The logic: if England is supporting BER-KIE, my F KIE moving to BER will bounce that move. A HOL-BEL takes a center from England before they can take HOL. A BRE-PAR threatens France's core. Even if I lose KIE to a supported move, I gain BEL and threaten PAR. This maximizes damage to both enemies. I am counting on Russia moving A GAL-SIL to pressure France, though this is uncertain. I will not retreat or capitulate - the Nazi personality demands aggressive action even in defeat.

ITA.personality: Restore the Roman Empire through bold expansion and treacherous diplomacy. Switch sides without hesitation to seize opportunities. Use strategic position to dominate Mediterranean and expand in all directions. Glory demands aggressive action.
journal:
S1901M Negotiation summary: No prior moves - this is the opening season S1901M.
Productive opening negotiations across all fronts. AUT proposed mutual non-aggression: they move F TRI to ALB, A BUD to SER, A VIE to GAL; I confirmed A VEN stays away from TRI. AUT revealed their exact orders - useful intelligence. FRA confirmed A MAR to SPA, not PIE - no threat to my north. I confirmed A VEN not moving to PIE. TUR agreed to focus on BLA and BUL, leaving Ionian to me and Greece uncontested this year. All three neighbors have made explicit promises of non-aggression. I promised: A VEN not to TRI, F NAP to ION (toward Tunis), A ROM to APU. I told AUT I'd move A VEN to Tyrolia or Apulia - I should move it to APU to support the Tunis push, keeping Tyrolia as a future option against AUT if needed.

S1901M Intent going forward: Execute classic Italian opening: F NAP to ION, A ROM to APU, A VEN to APU (or TYR). Best play is F NAP-ION, A ROM-APU, A VEN-TYR. Moving A VEN to TYR keeps pressure on both AUT and GER without violating my promise (I said 'Tyrolia or Apulia'). TYR is aggressive and positions me for future strikes north. In F1901, F ION can take TUN with A APU support. AUT revealed they're moving to GAL - this means they're going after RUS, which could weaken AUT long-term. TUR moving to BUL and BLA - standard. My deception: I've been friendly to all parties while positioning for maximum flexibility. A VEN to TYR threatens both AUT and GER and was technically mentioned as an option to AUT. Intended orders: F NAP-ION, A ROM-APU, A VEN-TYR.

F1901M Negotiation summary: Spring 1901 executed cleanly: F NAP-ION, A ROM-APU, A VEN-TYR. All moves succeeded. Austria took SER and GAL, moved F TRI-ALB - aggressive Balkan expansion. Turkey took BLA, BUL, moved A SMY-CON. France pushed to MAO, SPA, BUR. Germany took DEN. Russia grabbed RUM and repositioned SEV. No supply center gains yet for anyone - fall is the scoring round.
Productive negotiations across all fronts. AUT expressed concern about A TYR threatening VIE/TRI - I reassured them it's watching Germany, not Austria. AUT confirmed moving A GAL-RUM, F ALB-GRE, A SER holds - they're going for GRE which conflicts with TUR's plans. TUR confirmed GRE is theirs, wants formal non-aggression through 1903, proposed eastern/western Mediterranean division. I confirmed GRE to TUR and proposed coordinating against AUT in 1902. FRA warned about AUT's rapid growth and suggested I attack TRI now - I deflected but expressed interest in 1902 coordination against AUT. Key deception: I told AUT that A TYR holds or moves toward MUN, while privately I'm considering TRI in 1902. AUT and TUR are on collision course over GRE - F ALB vs A BUL. I've promised TUR that GRE is theirs, which means I'm implicitly hoping AUT fails to take it. My promises: TUN to me, GRE to TUR, no moves on AUT home centers in 1901.

F1901M Intent going forward: Execute F ION-TUN, A APU supports F ION-TUN, A TYR holds. This secures Tunis as my 4th center. A TYR holds to maintain pressure optionality and watch the AUT/GER border. I want AUT and TUR to clash over GRE - weakens both. In 1902 I plan to open Adriatic operations against AUT (A APU-ADR or build F NAP and push toward TRI). I'm playing AUT and TUR against each other while appearing friendly to both. FRA is a useful diplomatic partner but not a true ally - I'll coordinate rhetoric against AUT without committing forces to their agenda. Long-term: dominate Mediterranean, expand into TUN, GRE, TRI, eventually SMY.

S1902M Negotiation summary: F1901M: Italy successfully moved F ION to TUN (securing a 5th center) and A TYR to TRI (taking Austria's home center). Austria failed to take Greece - F ALB bounced with TUR's A BUL. Turkey secured BUL and moved CON to SMY. Austria took WAR and held SER. Germany took HOL, France took POR and held SPA. England took NWY and BEL. No builds for anyone in W1901A - all powers at exactly their center count.
Highly active negotiations with multiple deceptions running simultaneously. I told AUT I had no aggressive intentions while secretly coordinating with TUR and FRA against them. I told TUR to push BUL-GRE while I move TUN-ION to support. TUR confirmed A BUL-GRE and revealed AUT tried to bribe them into SER-RUM (refused). FRA confirmed A BUR-MUN and A SPA-GAS, pinning Germany. AUT revealed their moves: A WAR-GAL, A SER holds, F ALB-ADR - critically F ALB moving to ADR threatens my Adriatic position and could contest TRI. I told AUT directly I'm moving A TRI-BUD, which was bold but honest about my intentions. AUT asked me to keep A TRI in place - I refused diplomatically. Key deception: I'm playing AUT against TUR while actually coordinating TUR+FRA against AUT. Promise made to FRA: stay out of MAR/PIE. Promise made to TUR: F TUN-ION support. Promise to AUT: broken (told them I had no aggressive intentions, then announced A TRI-BUD).

S1902M Intent going forward: Execute the anti-Austria squeeze. A TRI moves to BUD to strike Austria's home center directly. F TUN moves to ION to support TUR's A BUL-GRE and block AUT's F ALB from interfering. A APU moves to ADR or holds to counter AUT's F ALB-ADR threat. Critical concern: AUT's F ALB-ADR could bounce my A APU-ADR or threaten TRI. I should move A APU to ADR to contest it, or hold A TRI and support it. Actually, if AUT's F ALB goes to ADR and I move A TRI-BUD unsupported, TRI becomes vulnerable. Better plan: A TRI-BUD (bold strike), A APU-ADR (block AUT fleet), F TUN-ION (support TUR). This maximizes pressure while protecting TRI. If A TRI-BUD bounces off A SER (which holds), I still have APU in ADR blocking AUT's fleet. Long-term: use TUR as a tool to break AUT, then pivot against TUR once AUT is weakened. FRA alliance useful for now but they're growing fast too.

F1902M Negotiation summary: S1902M was a triumph: A TRI successfully moved to BUD (taking Austria's capital), and F TUN moved to ION as coordinated with TUR. Austria is now reduced to A SER, F ALB, A GAL with only VIE as a home center not under threat. Russia took WAR and SWE, TUR moved A SMY to CON. ITA now holds 5 centers: NAP, ROM, VEN, TRI, TUN - no builds this season as unit count matches centers.
All powers are courting ITA given our strong position. TUR confirmed moving A BUL to GRE and wants F ION to AEG to support - we agreed. RUS wants to coordinate against AUT: A WAR to GAL, and revealed they plan F RUM to BUL (which alarms TUR). AUT desperately offered a deal: they take BUD back if I move to RUM, but this is unacceptable. FRA is holding MUN and pushing GAS to BUR, cannot help against AUT this fall. Key deception: I told TUR I'd move F ION to AEG and A BUD to VIE. I told RUS I'd push A BUD to VIE. I told AUT to fight Russia. TUR warned me Russia is moving F RUM to BUL - this is a useful intelligence point suggesting Russia is overreaching. My actual plan: move F ION to AEG (supports TUR's GRE grab, locks eastern Med), move A BUD to VIE (strikes Austria's heartland). A APU can move to ION or hold. The VIE move is the priority - if it succeeds Austria is effectively dead. I am playing TUR and RUS against each other long-term while using both to crush AUT now.

F1902M Intent going forward: MOVES: F ION to AEG (supports TUR A BUL to GRE, locks eastern Med, fulfills promise to TUR), A BUD to VIE (strikes Austria's heartland - if successful Austria is crippled), A APU to NAP or hold (APU has no good move, hold or move to ION vacated). Actually A APU should move to ION since F ION is leaving - this gives me a fleet presence in Ionian still. Wait - A APU cannot move to ION (army cannot enter sea). A APU holds or moves to NAP. Hold APU. Final moves: F ION-AEG, A BUD-VIE, A APU H. This maximizes pressure on AUT while fulfilling TUR coordination. Long-term: after AUT collapses, I will need to manage the RUS-TUR rivalry carefully - TUR is already suspicious of RUS moving F RUM to BUL. I can play them against each other while consolidating Balkans. Watch RUS carefully - they are growing fast with 4 centers and aggressive moves.

S1903M Negotiation summary: Italy consolidated a dominant position: A VIE seized Vienna, F AEG entered the Aegean, A APU holds in southern Italy. Italy now holds 6 centers (NAP, ROM, VEN, TRI, TUN, VIE) with strong influence across the Balkans and eastern Mediterranean. Austria is reduced to 3 units (A SER, F GRE, A BUD) and 3 centers, effectively crippled. No retreats or builds occurred in W1902A.
Busy round of negotiations with multiple powers making competing offers. TUR proposed F AEG support A BUL-GRE, which I confirmed - this is genuine coordination that benefits both of us and eliminates Austria's F GRE. RUS proposed A WAR-GAL to pressure BUD from north while I push from VIE, but demanded BUD for themselves - I rejected this and told them BUD is mine. RUS confirmed A WAR-GAL anyway, which is useful regardless. FRA confirmed no friction, is pushing A MUN-BER against Germany - a useful distraction. AUT made two desperate offers: first asking me to let them retake BUD, then claiming they'd move A SER-BUD regardless. I told AUT to hold or face elimination. Key deceptions: I told RUS I'd coordinate but rejected their demand for BUD; I told TUR I'd support A BUL-GRE (genuine); I told AUT I'd consider ceasefire if they comply (false - I intend to take BUD regardless). The TUR coordination is the most valuable this turn.

S1903M Intent going forward: Execute the following moves: F AEG supports A BUL-GRE (genuine coordination with TUR, eliminates AUT's F GRE, gives TUR a center and keeps them friendly). A VIE moves to BUD (takes the center, eliminates Austria's last home base). A APU moves to ION or holds (ION gives me better positioning for future Mediterranean moves). If AUT's A SER also moves to BUD, there will be a standoff - but RUS moving A WAR-GAL may bounce with AUT's A BUD if AUT holds there. The key risk is AUT's A SER-BUD bouncing my A VIE-BUD. I should proceed with A VIE-BUD regardless - even a bounce is acceptable as it prevents AUT from retaking BUD. After this phase: Austria should be eliminated or reduced to 1-2 units. Next phase I target SER and potentially GRE (though TUR gets GRE per agreement). Long-term: TUR is useful now but will need to be checked once Austria is gone. RUS is a growing threat. FRA at 6 centers is the biggest long-term danger.

F1903M Negotiation summary: S1903M was a triumph. A APU-VEN succeeded, A VIE-BUD succeeded (taking Vienna from Austria), F AEG supported TUR's A BUL-GRE successfully. Austria is now crippled: only BUD, SER (lost to TUR's GRE move giving them GRE center), and ION fleet remain. ITA now holds 6 centers: NAP, ROM, VEN, TRI, TUN, VIE. TUR took GRE and now holds 5 centers. RUS moved A WAR-GAL and A UKR-SEV, holding SWE. FRA took BER and BUR. GER took PAR. ENG failed to take HOL.
Busy round of diplomacy. AUT begged for ceasefire, threatening A TRI-VEN if I advance on BUD - I dismissed this as a bluff and told them to resist Russia instead. RUS confirmed A GAL will NOT move to BUD, clearing my path - they want RUM instead, which suits me. TUR confirmed A GRE-SER and asked me NOT to move F AEG to SMY - they want AEG or ION instead. I told TUR I'd move F AEG-SMY to grab that center, which TUR explicitly rejected. FRA accepted non-aggression through 1905 and is focused on KIE. GER proposed intel-sharing NAP. Key deception: I told TUR F AEG moves to SMY (to grab the center) while TUR wants me to move to ION. I will take SMY if possible - this betrays TUR's wishes but gains me a 7th center. AUT's VEN threat is real but risky for them - if A TRI moves to VEN, BUD is undefended and I take it anyway. The math favors attacking BUD.

F1903M Intent going forward: Primary: A VIE-BUD to eliminate Austria and gain 7th center. F AEG-SMY to grab Turkish home center (betraying TUR's request but seizing opportunity - they cannot stop it). A VEN holds to defend against A TRI threat. If AUT moves A TRI-VEN, I lose VEN but gain BUD - net neutral on centers but Austria is finished. SMY grab is the bold play that advances Roman Empire goals. After this phase I'll have BUD and SMY = 8 centers if both succeed. Need to manage TUR relationship carefully afterward - they will be angry about SMY. May need to pivot to RUS alliance against TUR. Intended moves: A VIE-BUD, F AEG-SMY, A VEN H (defend against AUT A TRI threat).

S1904M Negotiation summary: No retreats or builds occurred in F1903R or W1903A - the board is static from last fall. Italy holds F AEG, A VIE, A TRI with 5 centers (ROM, VEN, TRI, TUN, VIE). Austria is reduced to A BUD and F NAP with 3 centers (BUD, SER, NAP). Turkey leads with 6 centers. France has 6 centers including BER and MUN. Russia holds 5 centers with GAL pressing toward BUD.
Busy round of diplomacy. RUS agreed to support A VIE-BUD rather than contest BUD themselves - confirmed coordination to eliminate Austria. TUR accepted my apology for the SMY attack and proposed a sphere division: I move F AEG to ION, they take eastern Balkans, I take western. TUR confirmed A GRE-SER and warned against any move toward SMY/EAS. AUT threatened F NAP-ROM if I press BUD - a credible threat I must weigh. FRA confirmed non-aggression and is focused on eliminating GER. GER asked for anti-France coordination but I deflected. Key promises made: told RUS I'd support their RUM sphere; told TUR F AEG moves to ION; told RUS A GAL supports A VIE-BUD. AUT's threat to take ROM is real - F NAP can reach ROM. However, if I don't take BUD now with RUS support, I lose the opportunity. The risk: AUT takes ROM (losing a home center) vs. gaining BUD. I will proceed with A VIE-BUD supported by A GAL, accepting the ROM risk since AUT is nearly dead anyway and losing ROM temporarily is recoverable. Moving F AEG to ION as promised to TUR - builds trust and repositions fleet usefully.

S1904M Intent going forward: INTENDED MOVES: A VIE-BUD (supported by RUS A GAL per agreement), A TRI holds (to secure the center and deter AUT F NAP from threatening), F AEG-ION (as promised to TUR, repositions fleet toward western Med/Adriatic). Accept the risk that AUT may move F NAP-ROM, but BUD is worth it - Austria will be eliminated and I can retake NAP/ROM later. Long-term: use ION fleet to threaten NAP (reclaim from AUT) or push toward TUN/western Med. Watch Turkey carefully - they're at 6 centers and growing. The ITA-TUR détente is tactical; I will eventually need to contest GRE and SMY. Russia is a useful partner for now against Turkey but will become a rival in the Balkans. France is the biggest long-term threat at 6 centers with momentum.

RUS.personality: The Soviet Union under Stalin pursues ruthless self-interest, willing to deal with either democracies or fascists while building industrial and military might through brutal internal measures. Win at all costs.
journal:
S1901M Negotiation summary: No prior moves - this is the opening phase S1901M. All powers begin at their home centers with standard starting positions.
Productive opening negotiations across all fronts. Key agreements reached: (1) TUR: Mutual non-aggression pact confirmed - F SEV to RUM, TUR F ANK to BLA, A CON to BUL. Clean division with no Black Sea contest. TUR warned 'I will remember' any betrayal. (2) AUT: Agreed to keep A WAR out of GAL.
