My primary target is WAR (Austrian-held). I need you to hold BLA and keep pressure on AUT's southern flank via SER/BUL. Can A BUL move to GRE or SER to pressure Austria while I push from the north? Let's coordinate specific moves.

sent_missives.body: Turkey, we are aligned. Push A BUL to GRE this spring - I will move F TUN to ION to support your Aegean position and keep Austria's F ALB from interfering. I am pressing A TRI toward BUD or VIE simultaneously, squeezing Austria from both sides. Austria is trying to recruit me against you - I refused. Our coordination is the key to breaking Austrian dominance in the Balkans. Move decisively on GRE now while Austria is overextended.

Austria, I appreciate your proposal but I must be direct: your rapid expansion to 4 centers while holding WAR and SER makes you the board's dominant threat. I cannot ignore this. I am moving A TRI to BUD this spring - not as an enemy action, but to ensure balance of power. If you stand down from SER and return to a 3-center position, I am open to a genuine alliance against Turkey and Russia. Otherwise, I must act in Italy's interest. The choice is yours.

France, I am moving A TRI to BUD this spring to strike Austria directly. F TUN moves to ION to coordinate with Turkey against Austria's southern flank. I need you to push into MUN or at minimum hold BUR to prevent Germany from assisting Austria. If we both press simultaneously, Austria cannot defend everywhere. Stay out of PIE and MAR and I stay out of your sphere entirely - western Mediterranean is yours, I want the Balkans and Adriatic. Let's execute this together.

England — confirmed: A BUR moves to MUN this spring, A SPA to GAS. Germany is the target. I need you to move F BEL to HOL or support a push on HOL from BEL — that pins Germany from two directions simultaneously and we split the gains. You take HOL, I take MUN. If you hold BEL passive, Germany can concentrate against me alone. This is the moment to act — Germany at 5 centers with room to grow north is your problem as much as mine. Coordinate with me now or watch Germany consolidate.

Italy — good. A TRI pressing toward BUD or VIE is exactly right. I am moving A BUR to MUN and A SPA to GAS this spring — Germany will be under direct pressure and cannot assist Austria. Hold your commitment to stay out of MAR and PIE and I hold mine to stay out of your sphere. Austria pinned in the Balkans by you while I squeeze Germany from the west is the correct 1902 posture for both of us. Execute your Adriatic push — I will not interfere.

Germany — you did not respond last round, so I am acting on the assumption of hostility. A BUR moves to MUN this spring. This is not negotiable at this point — the window for a boundary agreement closed when you stayed silent. If you want to stop the bleeding in 1903, I am open to terms then. For now, England is pressing from the north and France from the west — you cannot hold both.

Germany, I'm with you on containing France. Here are my specific commitments this spring: F BEL holds in place to secure our shared border and deny France any northern advance. F NWY moves to NTH to give me flexibility and naval dominance. A YOR moves to LON to free up positioning. I will NOT move F BEL into HOL - that remains yours. In return, I need your assurance that A HOL does NOT move toward BEL. Push A RUH into BUR and let's see France squeezed from both sides. If you take BUR this spring, I'll be positioned to support a drive toward PAR in the fall. Agreed?

France, I hear your concerns about Germany, and I share them to a degree - but I must be honest with you: Germany has approached me with a similar anti-France proposal, and I am playing a careful game. My actual intention this spring is to hold F BEL defensively and move F NWY to NTH for naval positioning. I will NOT actively support Germany against you. However, I cannot commit F BEL to support HOL against Germany either - that would expose me. What I can offer: if Germany moves aggressively on BEL, I will defend it and that protects your northern flank too. Keep A BUR pressing MUN - a weakened Germany serves us both.

Russia, confirmed - Sweden is yours, no objection from London. F BOT to SWE is clean and I will not interfere. F NWY will move to NTH this spring, keeping well clear of your northern operations. I'd ask one thing in return: keep me informed if Austria or Turkey makes moves that could destabilize the east rapidly. A balanced east serves British interests as much as yours.

Comrade, let us finalize our coordination precisely. My moves this spring: A UKR - WAR (to retake my home center from Austria), F RUM supports A UKR if needed OR F RUM - BUL if you can vacate BUL first. I need you to move A BUL - SER to pressure Austria from the south and free BUL for my fleet. F BLA holds to secure our sea. A SEV holds to protect home. If you move A BUL to SER, I move F RUM to BUL - we both gain and Austria is squeezed from two directions simultaneously. This is the moment to strike Austria hard. Confirm A BUL - SER and I execute F RUM - BUL.

Austria, your offer is rejected. You propose I surrender RUM - a center I hold and fought for - in exchange for WAR which is already mine by right. This is not a negotiation, it is extortion. I am taking WAR back by force this spring. Your threat to coordinate with Turkey against me is hollow - Turkey is my ally and will not turn against me for your benefit. Vacate WAR peacefully and I will not press further into your territory. Resist and I will coordinate with Turkey to dismantle your Balkan position entirely. The choice is yours, but my army moves to WAR regardless.

Confirmed: F BOT moves to SWE this spring - Sweden is mine as agreed with England. Germany, I am pressing Austria hard on WAR this turn - expect Austria to be fully occupied on their eastern front, giving you freedom in the west. A strong Russia-Germany axis dividing Europe is exactly the right long-term arrangement. Keep France and England busy in the west while I handle Austria and Turkey in the east. No conflicts between us this season.

England, let's lock in specific moves. I will order A RUH - BUR this spring to strike directly at France's heartland. A HOL holds to secure our northern flank. F DEN moves to NTH to support your naval operations. I need you to move F BEL to support my A RUH - BUR, or at minimum hold BEL to prevent France retreating there. If we coordinate this correctly, I take BUR this spring and we can split France's remaining centers between us next fall - you take PAR and the Atlantic ports, I take MAR and the south. France's offer of a 'buffer' is stalling tactics - they have 5 centers and will only grow stronger if we hesitate. Strike now while they're exposed. Confirm F BEL supports A RUH - BUR.

France, your explanation of the MUN 'accident' is noted but unconvincing - armies do not accidentally march into enemy home centers. However, I am a pragmatic man. If you want peace, here are my terms: A BUR retreats to PAR or GAS immediately this spring, and France acknowledges HOL and BEL as German and English spheres respectively. In return I will not advance into BUR this season. But if A BUR remains in BUR threatening MUN, I will treat it as continued aggression and act accordingly with England's full cooperation. You have one move to demonstrate good faith.

Russia, understood on SWE - I have no objection to your fleet moving there, England may have views but that is between you and them. I am moving west against France this spring. One request: if Austria moves any units toward Silesia or Bohemia while I am engaged westward, I would appreciate a warning. Our mutual interest in a stable central Europe depends on Austria staying focused on the Balkans and not opportunistically stabbing northward. Keep pressing them in the east and we both prosper.

Comrade, let us be precise for this spring. I will move A BUL to GRE to secure that center, and F BLA will hold in the Black Sea to maintain our dominance there. I strongly recommend you move F RUM to hold or advance toward SEV while A UKR pushes to WAR to reclaim your home center from Austria - that is your priority target. A SEV can support F RUM or move to ARM. Austria is proposing I support A SER into RUM, which I am refusing - I will not betray our alliance. Hold RUM, take WAR back, and we squeeze Austria from both sides. Confirm you are not moving F RUM to BUL as that would conflict with my A BUL position.

Italy, confirmed - I am moving A BUL to GRE this spring. Your F TUN moving to ION is excellent and keeps Austria's F ALB pinned. I also have F BLA holding in the Black Sea. Austria is trying to bribe me into supporting A SER into RUM, but I am refusing - Russia is my ally and Austria is the threat. With you pressing from the Adriatic and me taking GRE, Austria's southern position collapses. Let's continue this coordination into summer - after GRE, I can push toward SER while you pressure TRI. This is the moment to break Austrian power in the Balkans.

Austria, I have considered your proposal carefully, but I cannot support A SER into RUM - Russia is my ally and I will not betray that relationship for a single center. However, I am taking GRE this spring with A BUL, and I have no designs on SER itself. If you keep F ALB out of the Aegean and focus entirely on your northern conflict with Russia, we need not be enemies. The choice is yours: fight me in the south while Russia presses from the north, or accept Turkish dominance of GRE and concentrate your forces where they matter.

Turkey, I accept your terms: Greece is yours, and I will keep F ALB out of Greek waters. My moves this spring: F ALB to ADR (away from your sphere), A SER holds, A WAR to GAL. In return, I need one concrete action from you: A BUL supports A SER into RUM, or at minimum A BUL moves to RUM directly to contest Russia. Russia holds both RUM and is pushing into WAR - they are the dominant threat to both of us. If we coordinate now against Russia, we both grow. If we let Russia consolidate, they will be unstoppable. This is the moment to act together. Confirm A BUL moves against RUM and we have a deal.

Russia, your ultimatum is noted. Here is my counter: I will move A WAR to GAL this spring, vacating Warsaw voluntarily - you can retake it uncontested. However, I will NOT simply hand you RUM as well. You already hold RUM, SEV, MOS, STP - four centers to my four. If you push into WAR while keeping RUM, you grow to five while I shrink to three. That is unacceptable. My offer stands: vacate RUM (move F RUM to SEV or BUL), I take RUM, you retake WAR, and we maintain parity and peace. If you refuse and attack, I will coordinate with Turkey and Italy against you immediately - I have already opened those discussions. The choice is yours.

Italy, I appreciate your cooperative stance. To be specific about my moves: A WAR to GAL, A SER holds, F ALB to ADR. I am NOT moving on Trieste or any Italian center - you have my word. Regarding your plan to move A TRI toward VIE or TYR: I must ask you to reconsider - moving toward VIE threatens my home center and would force me to respond defensively, damaging our relationship. Instead, keep A TRI in place or move it toward VEN to free up your forces. On Germany: I agree they are growing fast, but I cannot reach them easily from my position. Focus your pressure on Germany from the south while I handle the Balkans, and we both benefit. Let's not accidentally clash.

AUT.body: Austria, I appreciate your proposal but I must be direct: your rapid expansion to 4 centers while holding WAR and SER makes you the board's dominant threat. I cannot ignore this. I am moving A TRI to BUD this spring - not as an enemy action, but to ensure balance of power. If you stand down from SER and return to a 3-center position, I am open to a genuine alliance against Turkey and Russia. Otherwise, I must act in Italy's interest. The choice is yours.

Austria, your offer is rejected. You propose I surrender RUM - a center I hold and fought for - in exchange for WAR which is already mine by right. This is not a negotiation, it is extortion. I am taking WAR back by force this spring. Your threat to coordinate with Turkey against me is hollow - Turkey is my ally and will not turn against me for your benefit. Vacate WAR peacefully and I will not press further into your territory. Resist and I will coordinate with Turkey to dismantle your Balkan position entirely. The choice is yours, but my army moves to WAR regardless.

Austria, I have considered your proposal carefully, but I cannot support A SER into RUM - Russia is my ally and I will not betray that relationship for a single center. However, I am taking GRE this spring with A BUL, and I have no designs on SER itself. If you keep F ALB out of the Aegean and focus entirely on your northern conflict with Russia, we need not be enemies. The choice is yours: fight me in the south while Russia presses from the north, or accept Turkish dominance of GRE and concentrate your forces where they matter.

ENG.body: England — confirmed: A BUR moves to MUN this spring, A SPA to GAS. Germany is the target. I need you to move F BEL to HOL or support a push on HOL from BEL — that pins Germany from two directions simultaneously and we split the gains. You take HOL, I take MUN. If you hold BEL passive, Germany can concentrate against me alone. This is the moment to act — Germany at 5 centers with room to grow north is your problem as much as mine. Coordinate with me now or watch Germany consolidate.

Confirmed: F BOT moves to SWE this spring - Sweden is mine as agreed with England. Germany, I am pressing Austria hard on WAR this turn - expect Austria to be fully occupied on their eastern front, giving you freedom in the west. A strong Russia-Germany axis dividing Europe is exactly the right long-term arrangement. Keep France and England busy in the west while I handle Austria and Turkey in the east. No conflicts between us this season.

England, let's lock in specific moves. I will order A RUH - BUR this spring to strike directly at France's heartland. A HOL holds to secure our northern flank. F DEN moves to NTH to support your naval operations. I need you to move F BEL to support my A RUH - BUR, or at minimum hold BEL to prevent France retreating there. If we coordinate this correctly, I take BUR this spring and we can split France's remaining centers between us next fall - you take PAR and the Atlantic ports, I take MAR and the south. France's offer of a 'buffer' is stalling tactics - they have 5 centers and will only grow stronger if we hesitate. Strike now while they're exposed. Confirm F BEL supports A RUH - BUR.

FRA.body: France, I am moving A TRI to BUD this spring to strike Austria directly. F TUN moves to ION to coordinate with Turkey against Austria's southern flank. I need you to push into MUN or at minimum hold BUR to prevent Germany from assisting Austria. If we both press simultaneously, Austria cannot defend everywhere. Stay out of PIE and MAR and I stay out of your sphere entirely - western Mediterranean is yours, I want the Balkans and Adriatic. Let's execute this together.

France, I hear your concerns about Germany, and I share them to a degree - but I must be honest with you: Germany has approached me with a similar anti-France proposal, and I am playing a careful game. My actual intention this spring is to hold F BEL defensively and move F NWY to NTH for naval positioning. I will NOT actively support Germany against you. However, I cannot commit F BEL to support HOL against Germany either - that would expose me. What I can offer: if Germany moves aggressively on BEL, I will defend it and that protects your northern flank too. Keep A BUR pressing MUN - a weakened Germany serves us both.

France, your explanation of the MUN 'accident' is noted but unconvincing - armies do not accidentally march into enemy home centers. However, I am a pragmatic man. If you want peace, here are my terms: A BUR retreats to PAR or GAS immediately this spring, and France acknowledges HOL and BEL as German and English spheres respectively. In return I will not advance into BUR this season. But if A BUR remains in BUR threatening MUN, I will treat it as continued aggression and act accordingly with England's full cooperation. You have one move to demonstrate good faith.

GER.body: Germany — you did not respond last round, so I am acting on the assumption of hostility. A BUR moves to MUN this spring. This is not negotiable at this point — the window for a boundary agreement closed when you stayed silent. If you want to stop the bleeding in 1903, I am open to terms then. For now, England is pressing from the north and France from the west — you cannot hold both.

Germany, I'm with you on containing France. Here are my specific commitments this spring: F BEL holds in place to secure our shared border and deny France any northern advance. F NWY moves to NTH to give me flexibility and naval dominance. A YOR moves to LON to free up positioning. I will NOT move F BEL into HOL - that remains yours. In return, I need your assurance that A HOL does NOT move toward BEL. Push A RUH into BUR and let's see France squeezed from both sides. If you take BUR this spring, I'll be positioned to support a drive toward PAR in the fall. Agreed?

Confirmed: F BOT moves to SWE this spring - Sweden is mine as agreed with England. Germany, I am pressing Austria hard on WAR this turn - expect Austria to be fully occupied on their eastern front, giving you freedom in the west. A strong Russia-Germany axis dividing Europe is exactly the right long-term arrangement. Keep France and England busy in the west while I handle Austria and Turkey in the east. No conflicts between us this season.

ITA.body: Italy — good. A TRI pressing toward BUD or VIE is exactly right. I am moving A BUR to MUN and A SPA to GAS this spring — Germany will be under direct pressure and cannot assist Austria. Hold your commitment to stay out of MAR and PIE and I hold mine to stay out of your sphere. Austria pinned in the Balkans by you while I squeeze Germany from the west is the correct 1902 posture for both of us. Execute your Adriatic push — I will not interfere.

Italy, confirmed - I am moving A BUL to GRE this spring. Your F TUN moving to ION is excellent and keeps Austria's F ALB pinned. I also have F BLA holding in the Black Sea. Austria is trying to bribe me into supporting A SER into RUM, but I am refusing - Russia is my ally and Austria is the threat. With you pressing from the Adriatic and me taking GRE, Austria's southern position collapses. Let's continue this coordination into summer - after GRE, I can push toward SER while you pressure TRI. This is the moment to break Austrian power in the Balkans.

Italy, I appreciate your cooperative stance. To be specific about my moves: A WAR to GAL, A SER holds, F ALB to ADR. I am NOT moving on Trieste or any Italian center - you have my word. Regarding your plan to move A TRI toward VIE or TYR: I must ask you to reconsider - moving toward VIE threatens my home center and would force me to respond defensively, damaging our relationship. Instead, keep A TRI in place or move it toward VEN to free up your forces. On Germany: I agree they are growing fast, but I cannot reach them easily from my position. Focus your pressure on Germany from the south while I handle the Balkans, and we both benefit. Let's not accidentally clash.

RUS.body: Russia, confirmed - Sweden is yours, no objection from London. F BOT to SWE is clean and I will not interfere. F NWY will move to NTH this spring, keeping well clear of your northern operations. I'd ask one thing in return: keep me informed if Austria or Turkey makes moves that could destabilize the east rapidly. A balanced east serves British interests as much as yours.

Russia, understood on SWE - I have no objection to your fleet moving there, England may have views but that is between you and them. I am moving west against France this spring. One request: if Austria moves any units toward Silesia or Bohemia while I am engaged westward, I would appreciate a warning. Our mutual interest in a stable central Europe depends on Austria staying focused on the Balkans and not opportunistically stabbing northward. Keep pressing them in the east and we both prosper.

Comrade, let us be precise for this spring. I will move A BUL to GRE to secure that center, and F BLA will hold in the Black Sea to maintain our dominance there. I strongly recommend you move F RUM to hold or advance toward SEV while A UKR pushes to WAR to reclaim your home center from Austria - that is your priority target. A SEV can support F RUM or move to ARM. Austria is proposing I support A SER into RUM, which I am refusing - I will not betray our alliance. Hold RUM, take WAR back, and we squeeze Austria from both sides. Confirm you are not moving F RUM to BUL as that would conflict with my A BUL position.

Russia, your ultimatum is noted. Here is my counter: I will move A WAR to GAL this spring, vacating Warsaw voluntarily - you can retake it uncontested. However, I will NOT simply hand you RUM as well. You already hold RUM, SEV, MOS, STP - four centers to my four. If you push into WAR while keeping RUM, you grow to five while I shrink to three. That is unacceptable. My offer stands: vacate RUM (move F RUM to SEV or BUL), I take RUM, you retake WAR, and we maintain parity and peace. If you refuse and attack, I will coordinate with Turkey and Italy against you immediately - I have already opened those discussions. The choice is yours.

TUR.body: Turkey, we are aligned. Push A BUL to GRE this spring - I will move F TUN to ION to support your Aegean position and keep Austria's F ALB from interfering. I am pressing A TRI toward BUD or VIE simultaneously, squeezing Austria from both sides. Austria is trying to recruit me against you - I refused. Our coordination is the key to breaking Austrian dominance in the Balkans. Move decisively on GRE now while Austria is overextended.

Comrade, let us finalize our coordination precisely. My moves this spring: A UKR - WAR (to retake my home center from Austria), F RUM supports A UKR if needed OR F RUM - BUL if you can vacate BUL first. I need you to move A BUL - SER to pressure Austria from the south and free BUL for my fleet. F BLA holds to secure our sea. A SEV holds to protect home. If you move A BUL to SER, I move F RUM to BUL - we both gain and Austria is squeezed from two directions simultaneously. This is the moment to strike Austria hard. Confirm A BUL - SER and I execute F RUM - BUL.

Turkey, I accept your terms: Greece is yours, and I will keep F ALB out of Greek waters. My moves this spring: F ALB to ADR (away from your sphere), A SER holds, A WAR to GAL. In return, I need one concrete action from you: A BUL supports A SER into RUM, or at minimum A BUL moves to RUM directly to contest Russia. Russia holds both RUM and is pushing into WAR - they are the dominant threat to both of us. If we coordinate now against Russia, we both grow. If we let Russia consolidate, they will be unstoppable. This is the moment to act together. Confirm A BUL moves against RUM and we have a deal.

TUR.journal_summary:
F1901M: My A BUL attempted to take GRE but bounced with AUT's F ALB - neither of us got Greece. F BLA supported RUS's F RUM as agreed, maintaining our Black Sea alliance. A CON moved to SMY successfully. No builds in W1901A as all powers stayed at their center counts. I now hold BUL, BLA, SMY with 4 centers (ANK, CON, SMY, BUL).
Three-way pressure this round. AUT offered to concede GRE if I support A SER into RUM against Russia - a transparent attempt to use me as a weapon against my ally. I refused both times. RUS proposed F RUM to BUL if I vacate by moving A BUL to SER - a suspicious request that would put a Russian fleet in BUL, dangerously close to CON. I refused this too, telling RUS I'm taking GRE with A BUL and they should focus on WAR. ITA confirmed coordination: they move F TUN to ION, I take GRE with A BUL. ITA claims AUT tried to recruit them against me and they refused. Key deception: I told RUS I'm refusing AUT's offer to betray them, which is true, but I'm also not giving RUS what they want (A BUL to SER). I'm prioritizing GRE over helping either ally. AUT promised F ALB to ADR to stay out of my sphere - I'll see if they honor it. RUS's request for F RUM to BUL is alarming and suggests they may be planning to encroach on my sphere eventually.
TUR.intent: Execute A BUL to GRE - this is the priority. Greece gives me a 5th center and expands my Balkan footprint. F BLA holds to maintain Black Sea dominance. A SMY holds or moves to CON to free up flexibility. If AUT honors F ALB to ADR, GRE is uncontested. If not, I may bounce again - but ITA's F TUN to ION provides some pressure on AUT's southern flank. Long-term: after GRE, position to take SER or RUM. Watch RUS carefully - their desire to put F RUM into BUL is a red flag. The RUS alliance is useful now but they are a future threat to my Balkan expansion. ITA remains the most reliable partner for now. Intended moves: A BUL - GRE, F BLA H, A SMY H.

AUT.journal_summary:
F1901M: My A GAL successfully moved to WAR (taking a Russian home center), A SER held, but F ALB failed to take GRE as TUR's A BUL also moved there - a bounce. Italy took TRI with A TYR and TUN with F ION. No builds for anyone in W1901A as all powers ended at their starting center count (I have 4: BUD, VIE, WAR, SER).
Extremely difficult round. RUS issued ultimatum demanding WAR back, rejected my offer to swap WAR for RUM, and claims TUR is their ally. TUR confirmed they will take GRE with A BUL and refused to support A SER-RUM, citing Russian alliance. Most alarming: ITA threatened to move A TRI to BUD, calling me the 'dominant threat' - a complete reversal from our warm relationship. GER asked me to hold positions and not threaten their east. The Russia-Turkey alliance appears real and coordinated against me. Italy is now hostile. I am potentially facing a three-front threat: RUS from north, TUR from south, ITA from west. My promises: told TUR I'd move F ALB to ADR (away from Greece), told RUS I'd vacate WAR to GAL, told ITA I wouldn't move on TRI. I intend to keep the WAR-GAL move as it's strategically sound regardless. ITA's threat to take BUD is the most urgent crisis - I must defend BUD while managing the Russian pressure.
AUT.intent: Priority 1: Defend BUD from Italian A TRI. I must move A SER to BUD to block/bounce Italy's A TRI-BUD. This sacrifices Balkan momentum but BUD is a home center I cannot lose. Priority 2: Move A WAR to GAL as promised - this is strategically correct anyway (retreating from an exposed position, consolidating). Priority 3: F ALB to ADR as promised to TUR - keeps southern flank quiet and honors that commitment. The key deception: I told ITA I wouldn't move on TRI, but I'm moving A SER to BUD defensively, not offensively. This is defensive and justified given their threat. Intended moves: A WAR-GAL, A SER-BUD, F ALB-ADR. This defends BUD against Italy, consolidates my northern position, and keeps Turkey neutral. I lose the chance at RUM this turn but preserve my core. Relationship updates: ITA has turned hostile with BUD threat (-), RUS confirmed hostile (--), TUR revealed as RUS ally (-).

ITA.journal_summary:
F1901M: Italy successfully moved F ION to TUN (securing a 5th center) and A TYR to TRI (taking Austria's home center). Austria failed to take Greece - F ALB bounced with TUR's A BUL. Turkey secured BUL and moved CON to SMY. Austria took WAR and held SER. Germany took HOL, France took POR and held SPA. England took NWY and BEL. No builds for anyone in W1901A - all powers at exactly their center count.
Highly active negotiations with multiple deceptions running simultaneously. I told AUT I had no aggressive intentions while secretly coordinating with TUR and FRA against them. I told TUR to push BUL-GRE while I move TUN-ION to support. TUR confirmed A BUL-GRE and revealed AUT tried to bribe them into SER-RUM (refused). FRA confirmed A BUR-MUN and A SPA-GAS, pinning Germany. AUT revealed their moves: A WAR-GAL, A SER holds, F ALB-ADR - critically F ALB moving to ADR threatens my Adriatic position and could contest TRI. I told AUT directly I'm moving A TRI-BUD, which was bold but honest about my intentions. AUT asked me to keep A TRI in place - I refused diplomatically. Key deception: I'm playing AUT against TUR while actually coordinating TUR+FRA against AUT. Promise made to FRA: stay out of MAR/PIE. Promise made to TUR: F TUN-ION support. Promise to AUT: broken (told them I had no aggressive intentions, then announced A TRI-BUD).
ITA.intent: Execute the anti-Austria squeeze. A TRI moves to BUD to strike Austria's home center directly. F TUN moves to ION to support TUR's A BUL-GRE and block AUT's F ALB from interfering. A APU moves to ADR or holds to counter AUT's F ALB-ADR threat. Critical concern: AUT's F ALB-ADR could bounce my A APU-ADR or threaten TRI. I should move A APU to ADR to contest it, or hold A TRI and support it. Actually, if AUT's F ALB goes to ADR and I move A TRI-BUD unsupported, TRI becomes vulnerable. Better plan: A TRI-BUD (bold strike), A APU-ADR (block AUT fleet), F TUN-ION (support TUR). This maximizes pressure while protecting TRI. If A TRI-BUD bounces off A SER (which holds), I still have APU in ADR blocking AUT's fleet. Long-term: use TUR as a tool to break AUT, then pivot against TUR once AUT is weakened. FRA alliance useful for now but they're growing fast too.

GER.journal_summary:
F1901M: Germany successfully defended MUN against France's A BUR - MUN attempt (bounced by A RUH - MUN). A KIE - HOL succeeded, F DEN held. Net result: Germany holds 5 centers (BER, KIE, MUN, DEN, HOL) with units at F DEN, A RUH, A HOL. No builds in W1901A as all powers stayed at 3 units vs 3 centers. Austria took WAR and holds SER. England took NWY and BEL. France holds SPA, POR. Italy took TRI and TUN. Russia holds RUM.
England and Germany aligned on containing France - England commits F BEL hold, F NWY - NTH, A YOR - LON, and wants assurance HOL doesn't move toward BEL. I promised A RUH - BUR and F DEN - NTH. France attempted a peace offer (BUR as buffer) but when I didn't respond fast enough in sub-round 1, they declared hostility and announced A BUR - MUN this spring - a direct threat I must counter. Russia confirmed pressing Austria on WAR and moving F BOT - SWE, keeping Austria occupied. I told Russia I have no eastern ambitions. Key deception: I told France their terms were being considered while coordinating with England against them. France is now openly hostile. England is cooperative but I must watch HOL - they explicitly said they won't move there but that's worth monitoring. The England alliance is tactically useful but England at 5 centers is also a long-term threat.
GER.intent: PRIMARY: Counter France's A BUR - MUN threat. Move A RUH - BUR to bounce or take BUR (France moving out means BUR may be empty or I take it). If France moves A BUR - MUN, my A RUH - BUR takes BUR. A HOL holds to secure northern position and reassure England. F DEN - NTH as promised to England, giving naval flexibility and showing good faith. This gives me 6 centers if BUR falls. DECEPTION: Told France I might accept peace terms to keep them uncertain, but I am fully committed to taking BUR. WATCH: England at 5 centers growing - need to manage this alliance carefully and not let them get too strong. Russia moving to SWE is fine for now. Austria occupied in east is ideal. INTENDED MOVES: A RUH - BUR, A HOL H, F DEN - NTH.

FRA.journal_summary:
Fall 1901 resolved cleanly for France: F MAO took POR, A SPA held, A BUR attempted MUN but bounced against GER's A RUH. No builds for anyone — all powers ended at their starting count plus 1901 gains. France sits at 5 centers (BRE, MAR, PAR, POR, SPA) with A BUR well-positioned on the German border and F POR anchoring Iberia. GER secured HOL and DEN, ENG took BEL and NWY. Austria grabbed WAR and SER — four centers, the fastest grower on the board.
Three active threads this round. ENG was cautious but honest — admitted GER approached them with an anti-France proposal, confirmed they will hold F BEL defensively and move F NWY to NTH. They will not support HOL against GER but will not actively assist GER against France either. Useful but not a committed partner. ITA is fully coordinated: A TRI to BUD, F TUN to ION, pressing Austria hard. Promised to stay out of MAR and PIE — I reciprocated with a commitment to stay out of their sphere. This coordination is genuine and serves both parties in 1902. GER finally responded with an ultimatum: retreat A BUR to PAR or GAS or face war with England's cooperation. This is a bluff — GER cannot confirm ENG cooperation given ENG's actual communication to me. GER's demand that I retreat is unacceptable and confirms the hostile read. I told GER the window for negotiation closed with their silence. Promises made: told ITA I would push into MUN or hold BUR to pin GER — I intend to execute A BUR to MUN. Told ENG I would push A BUR to MUN — consistent. No promises broken this round. GER's claim of ENG cooperation is almost certainly false given ENG's candid message.
FRA.intent: Execute A BUR - MUN. This is the correct move regardless of GER's ultimatum — bouncing again achieves nothing, and a successful capture of MUN breaks GER open. A SPA - GAS repositions for future flexibility (MAR support, Iberian consolidation, or northern push). F POR holds — no fleet action needed in Iberia. If MUN succeeds, France jumps to 6 centers and GER is crippled. ENG holding BEL passively is acceptable — they are not actively hostile and GER-ENG coordination is likely overstated by GER. ITA pressing BUD pins Austria and prevents any eastern power from consolidating the center. Watch ENG carefully — if F NWY moves to NTH as stated, that is naval positioning that could threaten BRE or MAO in future seasons. Do not extend trust to ENG beyond the current non-aggression posture. Long-term: a weakened GER and pinned AUT in 1902 sets up France to dominate the western center by 1903. Intended moves: A BUR - MUN, A SPA - GAS, F POR H.

ENG.journal_summary:
F1901M went cleanly for England: F NTH moved to NWY, F ENG moved to BEL, A YOR held. Ended 1901 with 5 centers (EDI, LON, LVP, BEL, NWY). No builds needed. France also hit 5 centers (BRE, MAR, PAR, POR, SPA) after taking POR and SPA. Germany hit 5 (BER, KIE, MUN, DEN, HOL). Austria took WAR and SER for 4. Italy took TRI and TUN for 5. Russia took RUM for 4. Turkey took BUL for 4. France's A BUR attempt on MUN bounced with GER's A RUH. Austria's F ALB failed to take GRE as TUR's A BUL also moved there - bounce.
Both GER and FRA approached me with anti-the-other proposals, putting me in a classic pivot position. GER wants F BEL to support A RUH into BUR and promises A HOL holds (not threatening BEL). FRA wants F BEL to move to HOL or support HOL, promising to take MUN with A BUR. I told GER I would hold F BEL and move F NWY to NTH - no commitment to actively support BUR push. I told FRA I would hold F BEL defensively and NOT actively support Germany against them. I essentially told both sides I'm neutral-defensive while actually planning to move F NWY to NTH for naval flexibility. GER also revealed F DEN moving to NTH - potential conflict with my planned F NWY to NTH move. RUS confirmed F BOT to SWE, which I accepted. Key deception: I told GER I'm with them on containing France while telling FRA I won't actively support Germany. Neither gets what they want from me this turn. My actual moves: F BEL holds, F NWY to NTH, A YOR to LON. This keeps maximum flexibility - I dominate the North Sea and can pivot either way in fall.
ENG.intent: Execute: F BEL H, F NWY - NTH, A YOR - LON. Rationale: Holding BEL honors both promises (to GER and FRA) while moving to NTH gives naval dominance. A YOR to LON frees YOR for future use and consolidates home defense. I am NOT committing to either the Franco-German war on either side - I want them to bleed each other while I build naval strength. If GER's F DEN also moves to NTH, we bounce - acceptable outcome that still denies France NTH. In fall, I'll assess who is weaker and strike opportunistically. France at 5 centers with Atlantic ports is a long-term naval rival; Germany at 5 with HOL is a continental rival. Let them fight. Watch for GER F DEN moving to NTH - if so, I may need to reconsider NTH move but will proceed as planned. Russia taking SWE is fine - keeps them busy in north. Long-term: position for a strike on either HOL (from BEL) or PAR (naval) depending on who weakens first.

RUS.journal_summary:
F1901M: Russia held defensively across the board - F RUM held (supported by TUR's F BLA), F STP/SC moved to BOT, A UKR and A SEV held. Austria seized WAR with A GAL, moved A SER to hold, F ALB moved toward GRE but was contested by TUR's A BUL - GRE (result unclear, ALB ended in ALB, BUL ended in BUL based on current positions). Turkey supported RUM and moved CON to SMY. No builds for anyone in W1901A - all powers at same center count as units.
Key developments: TUR confirmed alliance but moved A BUL to GRE rather than SER as I requested, meaning F RUM cannot move to BUL this turn without conflicting. TUR explicitly warned me not to move F RUM to BUL. AUT offered a swap - vacate WAR voluntarily if I give up RUM - which I rejected firmly. AUT claims to be opening discussions with TUR and ITA against me, but TUR confirmed they refused AUT's request to support SER into RUM. ENG confirmed SWE is mine, F NWY moving to NTH. GER confirmed westward focus against FRA, asked for warning if AUT moves toward Silesia. My deception to AUT: told them Turkey is firmly my ally and won't turn - this appears true for now. TUR's move to GRE rather than SER is suboptimal for my plans but not a betrayal. I cannot take BUL this turn since TUR occupies it. Primary plan: A UKR - WAR to retake home center, F RUM holds or supports, F BOT - SWE for a free supply center gain.
RUS.intent: MOVES THIS TURN: A UKR - WAR (retake home center from AUT, who claims they'll vacate to GAL - if true, uncontested; if not, I bounce but lose nothing), F RUM - H or support A UKR if needed (cannot go to BUL due to TUR's A BUL still there moving to GRE, so BUL will be empty after TUR moves - but I cannot reach BUL from RUM in one move to an empty space without conflict risk), F BOT - SWE (free SC gain, ENG confirmed no interference), A SEV - H (protect home). Actually: if TUR moves A BUL to GRE, BUL will be empty - F RUM CAN move to BUL! This is a key opportunity. TUR warned against it but that was when they were still in BUL. If they vacate, I should take BUL. REVISED: F RUM - BUL (grab empty center if TUR vacates to GRE), A UKR - WAR, F BOT - SWE, A SEV - H. This gives me 6 centers if both WAR and BUL succeed. Risk: AUT may not vacate WAR, bouncing A UKR. Risk: TUR may not actually move to GRE. Fallback: if uncertain, F RUM holds and supports A UKR - WAR. Going aggressive: F RUM - BUL, A UKR - WAR, F BOT - SWE, A SEV - H. Long-term: squeeze AUT out of existence with TUR, then deal with TUR. Keep ENG and GER friendly as western counterweights.

sent_missives.body: The situation is clear: France occupies MUN with A MUN and has A GAS threatening further. I intend to retake MUN this fall - A BUR moves to MUN. I need you to hold F BEL firm and consider moving A LON to support pressure on France from the north. If you can push toward PAR or support my western flank, we can crack France open together. I'll keep A HOL in place to reassure you on HOL. What can you commit to against France this fall?

You sit in MY Munich with MY army. This is not a negotiating position - this is an occupation of German soil. Withdraw A MUN back to BUR immediately and we can discuss terms. If you refuse, I will retake MUN by force and you will find yourself fighting on two fronts. I have no interest in prolonged war with France if you respect German territory, but I will not tolerate this provocation. Your choice.

France has occupied Munich - a direct act of aggression against Germany. I am moving to retake it this fall. I want to confirm our non-aggression holds firm - I have no eastern ambitions and need my units focused west. In return, I will not contest SWE and will support your position against Austria if needed. Are we aligned for the foreseeable future?

Germany, the situation is clear: France has taken MUN and is growing dangerously strong at 5 centers with Atlantic access. I propose we coordinate directly this fall. I will move F BEL to HOL, taking it from you - but in exchange, I want you to support A BUR into MUN to retake it from France, and we split the spoils. Alternatively, if you prefer I support your A HOL to hold while you push BUR into MUN, I can do that too. Either way, France must be checked. What do you propose? I need a specific commitment from you this turn.

France, you've taken MUN which is impressive, but Germany will not let that stand - they will push BUR into MUN this fall with everything they have. I can help you hold MUN if you give me something concrete: promise me you will not contest HOL or move into the Channel, and I will use F BEL to support your A MUN against German pressure. A strong France holding MUN weakens Germany considerably, which serves both our interests. What are your planned moves this fall?

Russia, you've secured SWE and RUM - well played. I want to ensure we maintain our northern understanding. I have F NWY and you have F SWE - let's keep this border peaceful. I'm focused on the continent right now. Are you planning to push south toward TUR or west? I'd like to coordinate so we don't accidentally step on each other's interests in Scandinavia.

Comrade, our alliance has served us both well. This fall I intend: F RUM moves to BUL (now that your A BUL has moved to GRE, the center is open - I'm taking it), A WAR holds to secure my home center, F SWE holds, A SEV holds. I need your confirmation that A BUL did indeed move to GRE and BUL is vacant. If so, F RUM to BUL is clean. I also want to coordinate our next moves against Austria - your A CON and my forces can squeeze them from multiple directions. What are your intentions this fall? Let's divide Austria's centers between us.

Austria, your position is deteriorating rapidly. Italy has taken BUD, I hold WAR, and Turkey presses from the south. I offer you one chance at a negotiated arrangement: withdraw A GAL back to VIE and I will not press further into your home centers this turn. Refuse, and I coordinate with Italy and Turkey to eliminate you entirely. Your choice - cooperation or annihilation. I am a practical man and prefer a living Austria as a buffer, but only if you stop threatening my interests.

Italy, you hold BUD which is excellent. I hold WAR. Together we can dismember Austria completely - you take VIE and TRI, I take GAL. Austria has A SER, F ALB, and A GAL left - they are weak. Propose we coordinate: your A BUD moves to VIE, I move A WAR to GAL. Austria collapses. What are your intentions this fall and can we formalize this cooperation?

Excellent work this spring - we've both positioned well. I now hold BUD with my army, and my F ION dominates the Ionian. I propose we coordinate directly against Austria this fall: I will move A BUD to either VIE or SER, and F ION can move to AEG or support your push toward GRE. Austria's F ALB is isolated and vulnerable - if you move A BUL to GRE and I support from ION, we can crack them open. What are your intended moves? Let's divide the Balkans between us - I take VIE and BUD, you take GRE and BUL, and we squeeze Austria out of existence.

The spring went well for both of us - you're in MUN, I'm in BUD. Austria is being squeezed from multiple directions. This fall I intend to push A BUD toward VIE or hold to consolidate, while F ION threatens the eastern Mediterranean. I need you to maintain pressure on Austria from MUN - can you move A MUN toward VIE or BOH to prevent Austria from reinforcing? If we coordinate, Austria collapses within two seasons and we both gain. What are your fall intentions?

Austria, I'll be direct: I hold BUD and have no intention of returning it. However, I have no interest in destroying you entirely - a weakened Austria is more useful to me than a dead one. If you cede BUD formally and focus your forces on Russia (who holds RUM and WAR), I will support your eastern ambitions and not press further into VIE. Russia is the real threat to both of us. Consider: A GAL can push into WAR, F RUM is a Russian asset threatening your flank. Work with me against Russia and I'll stabilize your position.

Russia, the situation is clear: Austria holds SER with an army, has a fleet in ALB threatening Greece, and a unit in GAL threatening your position. I propose we coordinate directly this fall. I will move A BUL to GRE (finally taking it uncontested since AUT's F ALB moved to ADR last spring). More importantly, I want to discuss RUM - you hold it with F RUM, but Austria's A GAL is a threat. If you move F RUM to support against Austria, I can position A BUL to threaten SER next spring. Let's divide the Balkans: I take GRE, you keep RUM, we jointly pressure Austria. What are your specific moves this fall?

Italy, our coordination worked well last spring - you moved F TUN to ION as agreed. Now let's continue. I am moving A BUL to GRE this fall - with your F ION in position, AUT cannot contest it. Austria is the common threat: they hold SER, TRI (your home!), and have been aggressive in the Balkans. I propose we both pressure Austria - you push from BUD toward VIE or TRI, I take GRE and threaten SER. Austria cannot defend on two fronts. What are your intended moves this fall? I want to ensure we don't interfere with each other.

Austria, I am taking Greece this fall - A BUL to GRE. I have no interest in SER or RUM at this time. However, I notice Italy has taken BUD and is positioned aggressively against you. I could be persuaded to support your interests in the Balkans if you formally concede Greece to my sphere and keep F ALB away from the Aegean. Otherwise, I will coordinate with Italy and Russia against you. The choice is yours - make me an offer.

Italy, let's be direct: you took TRI and BUD, and I'm now squeezed from all sides. I have no interest in fighting you - that only benefits Russia and Turkey. I propose a clean division: you keep TRI and I keep BUD. I will move A GAL to BUD to reclaim it this fall - if you move A BUD elsewhere (say, toward RUM or SER), we avoid a clash and both gain. Russia holds RUM and WAR threatens my north; Turkey holds BUL and threatens my south. We have a common interest in not letting Russia dominate the east. I can offer you support into GRE or RUM if we coordinate. What do you say?

Russia, the situation has clarified considerably. Italy has seized BUD and TRI - they are now the dominant Balkan power, not me. I am fighting for survival against Italy from the west, Turkey from the south, and your pressure from the north. I propose a genuine ceasefire: I will not contest WAR or RUM this turn. In exchange, I ask you to hold F RUM in place and not advance on GAL. Italy is the real threat to both of us in the Balkans - A BUD gives them a foothold that threatens both our interests. Consider: a coordinated AUT-RUS push against Italy benefits us both far more than continuing to grind each other down.

Turkey, Italy now occupies BUD and TRI - they have 5 centers and are the strongest power in the Balkans. This threatens you as much as me. I propose we set aside our rivalry over GRE temporarily: I will move F ALB to GRE this fall if you support it, or we can coordinate A SER to BUL while you take GRE. Either way, we should be focused on containing Italy, not fighting each other. What specific moves are you planning this fall? Let's find an arrangement that benefits us both.

Germany, you now hold BUR and I hold MUN. This is a stalemate that benefits neither of us while England consolidates in the north and Austria grows unchecked in the east. I am prepared to offer you a clean partition: I keep MUN and push east toward BOH and SIL, you take BEL from England and consolidate the north. We both gain more from coordinating against England and Austria than from grinding against each other on the Rhine. If you reject this, I will hold MUN and support it from GAS — you will not retake it without English help, and England will extract a price for that help. The offer is on the table for this season only.

England, France now holds MUN and GER holds BUR — the German position is cracking. I intend to press east into BOH or BER this fall. This is the moment to coordinate: if you move F BEL toward HOL or press GER from the north, we split Germany between us and both gain centers. I am not moving against BEL or the Channel — my fleet is in POR and my armies are pointed east. What I need from you is confirmation you will not support GER's attempt to retake MUN. In return I will stay clear of BEL and NTH. Specific ask: hold BEL or move toward HOL, do not support GER into BUR or MUN.

Italy, your move into BUD is excellent — Austria is now squeezed between you and Russia. I am holding MUN and pressing deeper into Germany this fall. I need you to continue pinning Austria so they cannot reinforce the German theater. Specific coordination: keep A BUD in place or push toward VIE, and if F ION can threaten the Adriatic that keeps Austria's fleet options closed. I will stay entirely out of your sphere — MAR, PIE, LYO are yours. The western and central board is splitting cleanly between us. What is your intended move for F1902?

sent_missives.body: England, I reject your proposal to take HOL - that is a non-starter and you know it. Here is what I will do: A BUR moves to MUN to retake my home center. A HOL holds. F DEN moves to support or threatens SWE. I need you to hold F BEL firm and NOT move to HOL - that is the price of our cooperation. If you move on HOL while I'm fighting France, our alliance ends immediately and I will make accommodations with France against you. Hold BEL, let me retake MUN, and we coordinate against France together next season from a position of strength. That is the deal.

France, your offer is insulting. You want me to accept the permanent loss of Munich - a German home center - in exchange for attacking England? I will retake MUN by force this fall. A BUR moves to MUN. If you hold MUN with support from GAS, you will find yourself in a grinding war of attrition you cannot win while England presses from the north. Withdraw now and I will consider a ceasefire on the Rhine. This is your final warning.

Russia, France is attempting to permanently occupy Munich and is offering me a fantasy deal to distract me. I am retaking MUN this fall - A BUR to MUN. Our non-aggression stands firm and I appreciate your confirmation. Austria is stretched thin with your pressure from the east - keep it up. Once France is contained, Germany and Russia together can dominate the board. Stay the course.

Italy, I accept your framing - Russia is the primary threat. Here is my concrete proposal: I will move A GAL to BUD to reclaim it (you should move A BUD to RUM to take that center from Russia - F RUM is exposed and you can take it). F ALB moves to GRE. A SER holds. This way you gain RUM, I regain BUD, and we both expand at Russia's expense. I will not move on TRI or VEN. This is a genuine offer - let's execute it together this fall.

Russia, I hear your ultimatum but consider the reality: Italy holds BUD and TRI with 5 centers - they are the dominant power now, not me. I am moving A GAL to BUD to reclaim my home center from Italy. I am NOT moving on WAR or RUM this turn. F RUM is actually vulnerable to Italian A BUD - you should consider whether Italy is truly your ally or simply using you. I propose we both focus on containing Italy: hold F RUM where it is, and I will not threaten your positions. A living Austria as a buffer serves you better than an Italian-dominated Balkans.

Turkey, agreed - Greece is yours, I concede it formally. A BUL to GRE is fine by me. In exchange, I ask one thing: do not support Italy against me. I am moving A GAL to BUD to reclaim my home center from Italy. F ALB will move to GRE as well - if you're already moving A BUL there we'll bounce, but that's acceptable to me as it keeps the fleet away from your sphere. The real threat is Italy at 5 centers. Stay neutral between me and Italy this fall and I will formally recognize your sphere over GRE and BUL.

Confirmed: I will move F ION to AEG this fall to support your A BUL to GRE - Austria's F ALB cannot stop both of us. My A BUD will push to VIE to strike Austria's heartland. This is the killing blow: you take GRE, I take VIE, Austria is finished. Do not let Austria's F ALB escape to ADR - if it retreats there it threatens my position. Move A CON toward SMY or hold to free up your forces for the Balkan push. We are aligned - execute.

Russia, I accept your proposal in principle - Austria must be dismembered. I will push A BUD toward VIE this fall while you press A WAR into GAL. However, I want clarity on RUM: that fleet concerns me as it borders my sphere of influence in the Balkans. I propose you keep F RUM focused on BUL or the Black Sea, not the Adriatic or Balkan interior. If we coordinate cleanly - you take GAL and WAR, I take VIE and BUD - Austria collapses by 1903 and we can discuss a stable border. Agreed?

Austria, I appreciate your directness but I cannot accept your terms. BUD is mine and VIE is my next objective - this is the reality of your strategic position. However, I offer you this: if you move A SER to hold and A GAL to support against Russia, I will not press into TRI this fall. Your survival depends on choosing your battles - fight Russia in the east, not me in the west. A GAL pushing into RUM would actually serve both our interests and buy you time. The alternative is a three-front collapse.

Confirmed - my moves this fall: F RUM moves to BUL (taking the vacant center after your A BUL moves to GRE), A WAR holds, F SWE holds, A SEV holds. This gives me BUL and secures WAR. Next spring I propose: A WAR to GAL, F BUL supports or moves toward SER, your forces press from CON/GRE toward SER and VIE. Austria is finished. After Austria falls, we divide cleanly - you take SER, GRE, and the Adriatic coast; I take GAL, VIE, and BUD. This is the most efficient path to dominance for both of us. Confirm your A BUL is indeed moving to GRE this fall so I can execute F RUM - BUL without conflict.

Italy, I am moving A WAR to GAL this fall and F RUM to BUL - Austria will be squeezed from all sides. Your A BUD is perfectly positioned to take VIE next spring with my support from GAL. I propose we formalize: you take VIE and TRI (your home centers), I take GAL and WAR (my home centers). Austria is eliminated and we both grow. I have no interest in your Adriatic or Italian sphere - my focus is the Balkans and eastern Europe. Confirm you are pressing Austria this fall and we can coordinate the killing blow next spring.

England, Germany - our northern arrangement remains solid. I am focused entirely on Austria and the Balkans this fall. F SWE holds, no moves toward Scandinavia or the west from me. Germany, your non-aggression with me is confirmed - retake Munich from France, I have no interest in your western affairs. England, NWY is yours, SWE is mine, let's keep that border clean indefinitely. I will not interfere in your contest with France or Germany. We all benefit from stability in the north while I handle the eastern situation.

Russia, I must be direct with you: A BUL did NOT move to GRE last spring - it bounced with AUT's F ALB. BUL is still occupied by my army. F RUM moving to BUL would be moving into my occupied territory and I will not allow it. This would be an act of aggression against our alliance. My moves this fall: A BUL to GRE (finally uncontested since AUT's F ALB is now in ALB not GRE), F BLA holds, A CON moves to SMY. BUL will be vacant after I move to GRE - but I need your commitment that F RUM stays in RUM and does not take BUL. BUL is my center and I intend to keep it. In exchange, I will support your position against Austria - A BUL can threaten SER next spring. Do not move F RUM to BUL or our alliance ends immediately.

Italy, confirmed: I am moving A BUL to GRE this fall, F BLA holds, A CON to SMY. You push A BUD toward VIE, I take GRE and will threaten SER next spring. However, watch Russia - they just told me they intend to move F RUM into BUL (my center!) which suggests they are expanding aggressively westward. Russia with 5+ centers is as dangerous as Austria. Let's eliminate Austria first, then reassess. Confirm your moves so we don't interfere.

That is the best offer you will get from me.

Yes, you lose HOL temporarily, but you gain MUN back and we crack France open together. I will not support France's position in MUN - that is my firm commitment. France is the greater threat at 5 centers with Atlantic ports. Let's coordinate and divide their territory.

Hold MUN if you can, push east as you plan, and keep your forces away from BEL and the Channel as promised. We both benefit from a weakened Germany, but I must maintain my position carefully. Stay the course and we'll both profit.

Let's keep this border quiet and both expand in our respective directions.
